author: News18
summary: The Goa Government''s Smart Classroom initiative aims to transform education by teaching coding, robotics, and AI in schools. This program empowers students in government and private schools, preparing them for the future.
headline: Goa''s Smart Classroom Initiative Enhances Education with Coding, Robotics, and AI

author: News18
summary: Union Minister Nitin Gadkari claimed a “paid political campaign” against him over E20 petrol rollout is false. He urged the Finance Minister for GST relief for customers scrapping old vehicles to buy new ones, supporting consumers and the auto industry. His comments followed the Supreme Court dismissing petitions against E20. Gadkari also called for faster biofuel vehicle rollout and thanked automakers for transitioning to BSVI norms. He projected logistics costs to drop to 9% of GDP by 2025.
headline: Gadkari Defends E20 Rollout, Calls for GST Relief on Vehicle Scrappage

author: News18
summary: Vedhamoorthy, father of Sabareesan, passed away in Chennai after prolonged illness. He was 81. His body is at his residence for tributes, with last rites set for Friday.
headline: CM Stalin’s Son-in-Law Sabareesan’s Father Dies at 81 in Chennai

author: News18
summary: At News18 SheShakti 2025, women in sports, cinema, and music discussed building legacies. Kriti Sanon and Sanya Malhotra highlighted the impact of women-led stories in cinema. In sports, Mira Erda and Ashalata Devi addressed challenges like pay inequity and visibility. Kavita Krishnamurti emphasized talent, humility, and art''s role in nation-building. Together, they showcased women shaping Bharat''s future. For more, visit https://www.news18features.com/she-shakti-2025/
headline: Voices of Cinema, Sport, and Music at News18 SheShakti 2025 Redefine Nation-Building

author: Azhar Patangwala
summary: જીગીષા જાદવએ રજિસ્ટર વગર ગ્રો મની કંપની ખોલી 2.43 કરોડની છેતરપિંડી કરી. મહિલાએ 37 લોકો પાસેથી રોકાણ કરાવ્યું, પરંતુ વળતર ન મળતા લોકો પોલીસમાં ફરિયાદ કરી. તપાસમાં જાણવા મળ્યું કે આરોપી મહિલા અગાઉ શિક્ષક હતી અને ફ્રોડના પૈસાથી જમીન અને દાગીના ખરીદ્યા. પોલીસે રિમાન્ડ માટે કાર્યવાહી શરૂ કરી છે.
headline: ચાંદખેડા પોલીસે છેતરપિંડીના ગુનામાં જીગીષા જાદવને ધરપકડ કરી

author: Kaushal Pancholi
summary: અમદાવાદમાં પતિએ પત્નીનું જીવન નર્ક બનાવ્યું, પોર્નફિલ્મની આડમાં હિંસા કરી. બીજી મહિલા સાથેના ફોટા પરથી બીજા પતિએ પત્ની પર મારઝૂડ કર્યો. બંને મહિલાઓએ પોલીસ ફરિયાદ નોંધાવી છે.
headline: અમદાવાદમાં પતિ-પત્ની પર ઘરેલુ હિંસા અને અનૈતિક સંબંધોના બે ચોંકાવનારા કેસ

author: Azhar Patangwala
summary: ગુજરાતમાં ડિપ્રેશન હવે દક્ષિણ પૂર્વ પાકિસ્તાનમાં છે. 6 સપ્ટેમ્બરથી આ સિસ્ટમ ત્રીજી વખત વળાંક લેતી જોવા મળી. 11:30 વાગ્યે ભૂજથી 260 કિમી દૂર છે. હવામાન વિભાગ મુજબ આ સિસ્ટમ હવે દક્ષિણ-પશ્ચિમ તરફ જતી રહેશે, ગુજરાત તરફ ગતિ કરવાની સંભાવના નથી. 12 કલાકમાં નબળી પડી જશે અને 24 કલાકમાં ઉત્તર પૂર્વ અરબસાગરમાં ભળી જશે.
headline: ગુજરાતમાં ડિપ્રેશનમાં ફેરફાર, શું ફરી યૂ-ટર્ન લેશે?

author: Vipul Solanki
summary: બનાસકાંઠા અને વાવ-થરાદમાં 24 કલાકમાં 77 ઇંચ વરસાદથી ગંભીર પરિસ્થિતિ સર્જાઈ છે. સુઈગામમાં 410 mm વરસાદ પડ્યો, ઘણા ગામો સંપર્ક વિહોણા છે. સ્થાનિક તંત્ર દ્વારા રાહત કામગીરી શરૂ કરવામાં આવી છે. NDRFની ટીમે 250થી વધુ લોકોનું રેસ્ક્યુ કર્યું. 1678 લોકોને સ્થળાંતર કરાયું છે. હાલ 297 ગામમાં વીજ પુરવઠો પુનઃસ્થાપિત થયો છે.
headline: બનાસકાંઠામાં ભારે વરસાદથી સુઈગામ અને થરાદમાં જળમગ્ન, રાહત કામગીરી શરૂ

author: Azhar Patangwala
summary: ઉદ્યોગ મંત્રી બળવંતસિંહ રાજપૂતે ગુજરાત જનવિશ્વાસ (જોગવાઈઓના સુધારા) વિધેયક-2025 રજૂ કર્યું. આ વિધેયક 11 કાયદાઓ હેઠળ 516 જોગવાઈઓને અપરાધમુક્ત બનાવશે, જેમાં કેદની સજા દૂર કરવામાં આવી છે. આથી ઇઝ ઓફ ડૂઈંગ બિઝનેસને પ્રોત્સાહન મળશે અને નાની ભૂલો માટે ફોજદારી કાર્યવાહીનો ડર ઓછો થશે. વિધેયક બહુમતથી પસાર થયું.
headline: ગુજરાત જન વિશ્વાસ બિલ-2025 પસાર, 516 જોગવાઈઓમાં સુધારા થશે

author: Nirali Gohil
summary: કાઠમંડુમાં Gen-Z આંદોલન ભ્રષ્ટાચાર અને સોશિયલ મીડિયા પ્રતિબંધ સામે ઊભર્યું છે, 20 લોકોનાં મોત અને 400 ઘાયલ થયા. સરકારએ 26 પ્લેટફોર્મ પર પ્રતિબંધ મૂક્યો, જેના કારણે યુવાનોમાં રોષ ફાટી નીકળ્યો. ''હામી નેપાળ'' NGOએ આ આંદોલનને સંગઠિત કર્યું. સરકારના દબાવને સામે યુવાનો અભિવ્યક્તિની સ્વતંત્રતા અને ભ્રષ્ટાચારનો અંત માંગે છે.
headline: નેપાળમાં Gen-Z આંદોલન, 20 મોત, 400 ઘાયલ, સોશિયલ મીડિયા પર પ્રતિબંધનો વિરોધ

author: News18
summary: Shivam Raj from Gumla, Jharkhand, shows that a low Class 12 score doesn''t define one''s future. After scoring 74%, he regrouped, studied harder, and improved to 86%, successfully clearing JEE Main and JEE Advanced.
headline: From 74% to Rs 2 Crore Job: Shivam Raj's Journey to Google

author: Vipul Solanki
summary: ગુજરાતમાં 15મી વિધાનસભાનું સત્ર ચાલી રહ્યું છે. આજે પ્રશ્નોત્તરીમાં ધારાસભ્યોએ રાજ્યમાં એરપોર્ટ સ્ટેશનના પ્રગતિ અંગે પ્રશ્નો કર્યા. રાજ્ય સરકારે જણાવ્યું કે, કેશોદ, પોરબંદર, અને ભાવનગર એરપોર્ટના વિસ્તરણની પ્રગતિમાં છે. નવા ગ્રીનફિલ્ડ એરપોર્ટ માટે દ્વારકા અને દાહોદમાં જમીન મેળવવાની કામગીરી પણ ચાલી રહી છે.
headline: રાજ્યમાં નવા એરપોર્ટ સ્ટેશન, દ્વારકા અને દાહોદમાં પ્રગતિ હેઠળ!

author: Nirali Gohil
summary: હૈદરાબાદના સુજોક થેરાપી માસ્ટર જીજ્ઞેશભાઈ ગોકાણીના માર્ગદર્શન હેઠળ રાજકોટને પેઈન ફ્રી બનાવવાનો પ્રોજેક્ટ શરૂ થયો છે. આમાં 40થી વધુ આયુર્વેદિક ડોક્ટર્સે ભાગ લીધો. સુજોક થેરાપી, જે હાથ અને પગના ચોક્કસ બિંદુઓ પર દબાણથી થાય છે, દર્દમાં તરત રાહત આપે છે. સૌરાષ્ટ્ર યુનિવર્સિટી સાથેના એમઓયુ હેઠળ આ પદ્ધતિ શીખવા માટે લોકો જોડાઈ શકે છે.
headline: રાજકોટને પેઈન ફ્રી બનાવશે સુજોક થેરાપી, જાણો ફાયદાઓ

author: Kaushal Pancholi
summary: ગુજરાતમાં બંગાળની ખાડીની સિસ્ટમ ધીમે ધીમે દૂર જઈ રહી છે. હવામાન વિભાગે 9થી 15મી સપ્ટેમ્બર સુધી હળવાથી મધ્યમ વરસાદની આગાહી કરી છે. આજે કચ્છમાં ભારે વરસાદની આગાહી છે, માછીમારોને દરિયો ન ખેડવાની સલાહ આપવામાં આવી છે. દરિયાકાંઠે 45થી 55 કેએમપીએચની હવા ફૂંકાશે, મહત્તમ સ્પીડ 65 સુધી જઈ શકે છે.
headline: ગુજરાતમાં 15મી સપ્ટેમ્બર સુધીમાં વરસાદની આગાહી, કચ્છમાં ભારે વરસાદ

author: Azhar Patangwala
summary: ઉપરવાસમાં વધુ વરસાદને કારણે સાબરમતી નદીમાં પાણી છોડવાની ફરજ પડી. ખેડા જિલ્લામાં વાત્રક નદીમાં પૂરની સ્થિતિ સર્જાઈ છે, જે 10000 હેક્ટર જમીનને અસર કરી રહી છે. ખેડા તાલુકાના રસિકપુરા ગામમાં પણ નદીના પાણીથી ખેતરોમાં મોટાપાયે ધોવાણ થયું છે, જેના કારણે ખેડૂતોને ભારે નુકસાન થયું છે.
headline: ખેડા જિલ્લામાં વાત્રક નદીમાં પૂરની સ્થિતિ, ખેડૂતોને નુકસાન

author: Nirali Gohil
summary: બનાસકાંઠામાં 3 દિવસથી ભારે વરસાદમાં 133 ગામોમાં વીજ પુરવઠો પુનઃસ્થાપિત થયો છે. વરસાદથી અનેક ગામોમાં પાણી ભરાયા, રસ્તાઓ ધોવાયા, અને નાગરિકોને મુશ્કેલીનો સામનો કરવો પડ્યો. કલેકટર મિહિર પટેલના નેતૃત્વમાં રાહત કામગીરી શરૂ થઈ છે. 9 સપ્ટેમ્બર 2025ના 10 વાગ્યા સુધીમાં 297 ગામોમાંથી 133માં વીજ પુરવઠો ફરી કાર્યરત કરવામાં આવ્યો છે.
headline: બનાસકાંઠામાં ભારે વરસાદ બાદ 133 ગામોમાં વીજ પુરવઠો પુનઃસ્થાપિત

author: Sarman Ram
summary: મહેસાણાના વિઠોડા ગામની અનુપમ પ્રાથમિક શાળાએ 300 વિદ્યાર્થીઓના અક્ષરમાં સુધારો કર્યો છે. શિક્ષક આદિત્ય દરજીએ 20 દિવસમાં આ સફળતા પ્રાપ્ત કરી, જેના પરિણામે રાજ્ય કક્ષાએ શ્રેષ્ઠ શિક્ષક એવોર્ડ મળ્યો. શાળાએ NEP-2020ના ધ્યેયોને અનુસરીને સર્વાંગી વિકાસ પર ભાર મૂક્યો છે, અને GCERTએ આ પ્રયોગનો વીડિયો બનાવ્યો છે. 444 વિદ્યાર્થીઓ અહીં અભ્યાસ કરે છે.
headline: મહેસાણાની શાળા 300 બાળકોના અક્ષરમાં સુધારો, શ્રેષ્ઠ શિક્ષક એવોર્ડ જીત્યો

author: News18
summary: Amit Shah inaugurated the Fast Track Immigration – Trusted Traveller Programme at five airports via video link. This program is for Indian citizens and OCI cardholders, allowing faster immigration with automated e-gates. He also honored Swami Vivekananda on the anniversary of his Chicago speech, emphasizing the pride in this initiative.
headline: Amit Shah Launches Fast-Track Immigration at 5 Airports

author: News18
summary: A 50-year-old woman, Renu Agarwal, was found dead in her Kukatpally home with stab wounds on Wednesday. Assailants tied her up, stabbed her, and used a pressure cooker in the attack. They fled with cash and gold after showering to erase evidence. Police suspect a house help and a friend involved, and investigations are ongoing, including fingerprint analysis and CCTV review.
headline: Thieves Kill Woman in Hyderabad, Flee After Showering

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court declined to urgently list a petition against the Asia Cup match on September 14. Justices J K Maheshwari and Vijay Bishnoi questioned the urgency, stating, “Match should go on.” The plea, by law students, argued the match sends a negative message post-Pahalgam attack, impacting national dignity and sentiments of victims'' families. They claimed it undermines national interests and morale of armed forces.
headline: SC Rejects Plea to Cancel India-Pakistan Cricket Match, Says '

author: News18
summary: Tensions rise along the India-Nepal border after jailbreaks in Nepal. In Bihar''s Sitamarhi, 21 were detained as prisoners from Jaleshwar jail attempted to enter India. The Sashastra Seema Bal and local police increased vigilance. Reports indicate around 7,000 prisoners escaped in several incidents across Nepal amid protests. Authorities are on high alert, with checks on vehicles and buses in the border areas.
headline: India Enhances Border Security After Nepal Jailbreaks, 21 Detained in Bihar

author: News18
summary: BJP criticized Congress leader Udit Raj for his '
created_by: where protests have led to significant unrest and casualties. BJP spokesperson CR Kesavan condemned the remarks
headline: claiming they incite unrest and threaten democracy. Meanwhile
image_url: Nepal''s Mayor urged calm as the country navigates a transition to interim governance and upcoming elections."
source_url: BJP Condemns Udit Raj's 'Anti-National' Remarks on Nepal Situation

author: News18
summary: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge urged Gujarat leaders to “remove immediately” non-performing members. At a training camp in Junagadh, he emphasized grassroots strength and discipline, citing an internal report indicating 19 out of 41 units lagging. Kharge''s comments align with Congress''s stance on dismissing compromised leaders, following Rahul Gandhi''s remarks on maintaining integrity within the party.
headline: Kharge Urges Gujarat Congress to Remove 'Bad Mangoes' to Strengthen Party

author: News18
summary: The Delhi court reserved its verdict on charges against Lalu Prasad Yadav and others in the land-for-job case. The CBI claims sufficient evidence exists, while Yadav''s lawyer argues the case is politically motivated and lacks proof of wrongdoing, stating no jobs were exchanged for land. The defense contends all transactions were legitimate and independent, with no corruption demonstrated.
headline: Delhi Court Reserves Verdict on Lalu Prasad Yadav's Land-For-Job Scam Charges

author: News18
summary: At News18 SheShakti 2025, women leaders discussed overcoming challenges and empowering communities. Shanti Raghavan emphasized inclusion for people with disabilities. Mentorship was highlighted by Lakshmi V. Venkatesan, resonating with others on creating ecosystems for women''s success. Conversations on culture showed media''s role in shaping perceptions. Together, these leaders demonstrated that SheShakti is a collective force for building New Bharat.
headline: Women Leaders at News18 SheShakti 2025 Redefine Nation Building

author: News18
summary: Sushila Karki, Nepal’s first woman Chief Justice, reflects on her time at Banaras Hindu University (BHU) as Gen Z protesters choose her to lead Nepal. Born in 1952, she completed her master''s in political science at BHU in 1975, where she met her future husband. Karki''s academic journey and commitment to integrity shaped her career, especially during her tenure as Chief Justice, where she faced an impeachment motion that was later withdrawn. BHU plans to archive her memories as a point of pride.
headline: Sushila Karki: From BHU Student to Nepal's First Woman Chief Justice

author: News18
summary: Union Railways Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw announced a daily train from Kashmir to Delhi starting September 13. This follows the partial reopening of the Srinagar-Jammu National Highway, which had been closed due to landslides. Growers reported losses of ₹500 to 700 crore as apples rotted in trucks waiting for clearance. September marks the peak apple harvesting season in Kashmir.
headline: Daily Parcel Train for Kashmir Apples to Start September 13

author: Shikhar Shukla
summary:
हैदराबाद: मूसराबाद इलाके में बने अरबियन मण्डी रेस्तरां में एक हैरान करने वाली घटना सामने आई. यहां बिरयानी खाने आए एक ग्राहक की थाली में मरा हुआ कॉकरोच निकला. ग्राहक का कहना है कि वह रेस्तरां में दोस्तों के साथ खाना खा रहा था, तभी उसकी नजर बिरयानी की प्लेट में पड़े कॉकरोच पर पड़ी.
ग्राहक ने तुरंत ही इस पूरी घटना का वीडियो बना लिया और रेस्तरां स्टाफ से शिकायत की. लेकिन शिकायत को गंभीरता से लेने की बजाय मैनेजमेंट ने मामले को टालने की कोशिश की. यही बात ग्राहक को नागवार गुजरी और फिर दोनों पक्षों में बहस शुरू हो गई.
पुलिस पहुंची लेकिन मामला ठंडा
विवाद बढ़ता देख पुलिस को मौके पर बुलाया गया. पुलिस कर्मियों ने दोनों पक्षों से बात की, मगर इस पूरे मामले में अभी तक कोई औपचारिक केस दर्ज नहीं हुआ है. फिलहाल पुलिस का रुख नरम है, लेकिन सोशल मीडिया पर इस घटना को लेकर माहौल गर्म है.
सोशल मीडिया पर गुस्सा
ग्राहक द्वारा बनाया गया वीडियो अब सोशल मीडिया पर खूब वायरल हो रहा है. वीडियो में साफ दिखाई देता है कि बिरयानी की प्लेट में कॉकरोच पड़ा है और ग्राहक स्टाफ से बहस कर रहा है. वीडियो सामने आते ही नेटिजन्स ने रेस्तरां पर सवाल उठाने शुरू कर दिए.
कई लोगों ने उन इन्फ्लुएंसर्स को भी कटघरे में खड़ा किया जो इस तरह के रेस्तरां की लगातार तारीफ करते रहते हैं. X पर एक यूजर ने लिखा – ‘लोगों को ऐसे रेस्तरां का प्रचार करने वाले इन्फ्लुएंसर्स से भी सवाल करना चाहिए.‘
रेस्तरां पर पहले भी लगे आरोप
अरबियन मण्डी का नाम इससे पहले भी विवादों में आ चुका है. इसी साल फरवरी में तेलंगाना फूड सेफ्टी कमिश्नर की टास्क फोर्स ने इस चेन के सैनीकपुरी ब्रांच पर छापा मारा था. वहां की किचन में गंदगी, कॉकरोच, खराब सामान और गंदे फ्रिज मिले थे. अधिकारियों ने उस समय साफ कहा था कि यहां की हालत बेहद खराब है और फूड सेफ्टी एंड स्टैंडर्ड्स एक्ट, 2006 के तहत कार्रवाई होगी.
साख पर सवाल और बढ़ी मुश्किलें
अब मूसराबाद वाले मामले ने रेस्तरां की साख पर और चोट पहुंचाई है. लोग सोशल मीडिया पर लिख रहे हैं कि जब इतनी शिकायतें मिल चुकी हैं तो आखिर इस रेस्तरां को बंद क्यों नहीं किया जाता. कई ग्राहकों ने पुराने अनुभव भी शेयर किए, जिनमें उन्होंने लिखा कि उन्हें भी यहां की साफ-सफाई पर संदेह था.
ग्राहकों की मांग, सख्ती से जांच हो
इस पूरे विवाद के बाद अब आवाजें उठ रही हैं कि शहर के सभी होटल और रेस्तरां की नियमित जांच होनी चाहिए. एक्टिविस्ट्स और ग्राहक संगठनों का कहना है कि सिर्फ अरबियन मण्डी ही नहीं, बल्कि हर बड़े-छोटे रेस्तरां को नियमों के हिसाब से जांचना चाहिए.
headline: बिरयानी में ढूंढ रहे थे ''''पीस'''', थाली के बीच से कुछ ऐसा निकला..देखते ही मचा बवाल

author: News18
summary: The MEA has addressed reports of Indians joining Russia''s war, urging nationals to avoid such offers. A spokesperson highlighted the risks involved and confirmed discussions with Russian authorities in Delhi and Moscow to end this practice. The warning follows claims by two Indian men in Ukraine who were lured with job offers but sent to the frontline instead. They traveled on visas and were misled by an agent promising construction work.
headline: MEA Warns Indians Against Joining Russian Army Amid Recruitment Reports

author: News18
summary: PM Modi shared Swami Vivekananda''s 1893 speech from Chicago, calling it a celebrated moment in history. Delivered at the World’s Parliament of Religions, Vivekananda emphasized harmony and religious tolerance, addressing the need for acceptance among all faiths. His message remains relevant today, highlighting the importance of unity and understanding in a divided world.
headline: PM Modi Celebrates Swami Vivekananda’s Iconic 1893 Chicago Speech

author: News18
summary: Karnataka Home Minister G Parameshwara attended an ABVP event in Tiptur, Tumakuru, for Rani Abbakka''s 500th anniversary. This follows Deputy CM DK Shivakumar''s controversy over singing the RSS anthem in the assembly during a debate on August 21 regarding a stampede at M Chinnaswamy stadium.
headline: Karnataka Home Minister Attends ABVP Event Amid RSS Anthem Controversy

author: News18
summary: PM Modi met Mauritius PM Navinchandra Ramgoolam in Varanasi on September 11, 2025, for bilateral talks. Ramgoolam is on an eight-day visit to India. The leaders greeted each other warmly, and after the meeting, the delegation will witness the Ganga Aarti and visit Kashi Vishwanath temple. Varanasi is decorated to welcome the Mauritian PM, with enhanced security in place.
headline: PM Modi Meets Mauritius PM Ramgoolam in Varanasi for Bilateral Talks

author: News18
summary: A report shows cyber fraudsters are profiling and targeting elderly citizens, especially from modest backgrounds. Victims across India, particularly in Southern cities, have lost significant amounts. Cases include a 64-year-old in Mohali losing Rs 1.5 crore and a 78-year-old in Telangana who lost Rs 30 lakh. The report notes a 400% rise in cybercrime incidents, with fraudsters exploiting seniors'' financial vulnerabilities and limited digital awareness.
headline: Cyber Fraud Targets India''s Elderly, Reveals Government Report

author: News18
summary: Over 200 passengers were deplaned from Air India flight AI2380 at Delhi airport due to a technical glitch. They were seated for about two hours before being offloaded due to faulty air conditioning. The flight, bound for Singapore, was delayed and had issues with the electric supply. Passengers were taken to the terminal, but Air India has not issued an official statement regarding the incident.
headline: Air India Flight Delays Over 200 Passengers for 2 Hours Due to Technical Issue

author: News18
summary: BMTC, Bengaluru''s key transport, faces over 10,000 complaints from January to September, surpassing last year''s total. An average of 100 complaints daily highlights growing dissatisfaction with the service.
headline: Over 10,000 Complaints Filed Against BMTC in 2023

author: News18
summary: India is closely watching the unrest in Nepal, prioritizing border security to prevent disturbances. Intelligence sources warn that unrest could impact Indian states with ties to Nepal, leading to illegal activities. New Delhi aims to avoid interference while safeguarding its interests, preferring a leadership in Nepal that balances youth and traditional institutions. Indian agencies emphasize the importance of ties with the Nepal Army amid concerns over stability.
headline: India Monitors Nepal Unrest, Focuses on Border Security

author: News18
summary: Thiruvananthapuram International Airport''s expansion will increase terminal capacity fivefold and extend the runway by 145 meters. Set to begin by year-end, the Rs 1,300 crore phase one aims to reduce congestion and improve operations. The airport currently serves 4.5 million passengers annually, with footfall at 4.9 million in 2024-2025. A new apron building and upgraded infrastructure are also planned, with completion expected in five years.
headline: Thiruvananthapuram Airport Expansion Plan: Rs 1,300 Crore Investment

author: Santosh Kumar
summary:
West Bengal News: पश्चिम बंगाल की सियासत में एक बार फिर बंगाली बनाम बाहरी का मुद्दा गरमाता दिख रहा है. केंद्रीय मंत्री और भारतीय जनता पार्टी के नेता सुकांत मजूमदार ने मुख्यमंत्री ममता बनर्जी पर तीखा हमला बोला है. मजूमदार ने ममता के हालिया बयान के जवाब में कहा कि बंगाल को चलाने के लिए बंगाली तैयार है. आपके जाने का समय आ गया है. आपने बंगालियों की जितनी हानि की है, उतनी हानि बंगाल की किसी ने नहीं की. उन्होंने टीएमी सुप्रीमो पर निशाना साधते हुए यह भी कहा कि अगर बंगालियों का आपको इतना ही ख्याल होता तो आप यूसुफ पठान को यहां नहीं लाती.
मजूमदार का यह बयान टीएमसी के बहरमपुर से सांसद और पूर्व क्रिकेटर यूसुफ पठान को लेकर है, जिन्हें बीजेपी ने बाहरी बताकर ममता बनर्जी पर हमला करने का हथियार बनाया है. पठान गुजरात से हैं. उनको 2024 के लोकसभा चुनाव में टीएमसी ने बहरमपुर से उम्मीदवार बनाया था और उनकी जीत ने बंगाल की सियासत में नया रंग भरा. बीजेपी इस मुद्दे को भुनाने की कोशिश में है, ताकि ममता बनर्जी की बंगाली अस्मिता की छवि को चुनौती दी जा सके.
सुकांत मजूमदार पिछले कुछ समय से लगातार “बंगाली बनाम बाहरी” के मुद्दे को हवा दे रहे हैं. उनका दावा है कि ममता बनर्जी की नीतियां बंगाल के स्थानीय लोगों के हितों के खिलाफ हैं और TMC बाहरी लोगों को बढ़ावा दे रही है. यह रणनीति बीजेपी की उस कोशिश का हिस्सा है, जिसमें वह बंगाल में अपनी पैठ मजबूत करने के लिए स्थानीय भावनाओं को उभारना चाहती है.
टीएमसी ने सस्ती राजनीति करार दिया
दूसरी ओर टीएमसी ने मजूमदार के बयान को खारिज करते हुए इसे सस्ती राजनीति करार दिया है. पार्टी के एक वरिष्ठ नेता ने कहा कि ममता बनर्जी ने हमेशा बंगाल की संस्कृति और अस्मिता को प्राथमिकता दी है. यूसुफ पठान जैसे लोग बंगाल की जनता की सेवा के लिए चुने गए हैं, न कि किसी बाहरी एजेंडे के लिए. टीएमसी का तर्क है कि BJP इस तरह के बयानों से केवल सामाजिक विभाजन पैदा करना चाहती है.
राजनीतिक विश्लेषकों का मानना है कि बीजेपी की यह रणनीति 2026 के विधानसभा चुनावों से पहले ममता बनर्जी को घेरने की कोशिश है. बंगाल में बाहरी का मुद्दा हमेशा से संवेदनशील रहा है और बीजेपी इसे हवा देकर टीएमसी के वोट बैंक में सेंध लगाने की कोशिश कर रही है. हालांकि, ममता बनर्जी की बंगाली अस्मिता की छवि और उनके मजबूत जनाधार को तोड़ना बीजेपी के लिए आसान नहीं होगा. इस सियासी जंग में यूसुफ पठान का नाम एक नए विवाद के रूप में उभरा है. सवाल यह है कि क्या बीजेपी की यह रणनीति बंगाल की जनता को प्रभावित कर पाएगी या ममता बनर्जी इस हमले को अपनी सियासी चतुराई से नाकाम कर देंगी?
headline: बंगाल को बंगाली चलाएंगे... यूसुफ को हथियार बना ममता को बोल्ड करने में जुटी BJP

author: News18
summary: The Modi government reduced GST on critical medicines, dropping rates from 12% to 5% or zero. This change lowers costs for treatments for cancer and rare diseases, including the costly Zolgensma gene therapy, which sees a price drop of over Rs 1.1 crore. Other drugs like Atezolizumab and Alectinib also became cheaper. Experts say this move improves accessibility and affordability for patients facing high medical bills.
headline: GST Cuts Make Life-Saving Drugs for Cancer and Rare Diseases More Affordable

author: News18
summary: The Maharashtra government proposed moving the Dahisar Toll Plaza near Versova Bridge to ease traffic on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad Highway. This decision aims to reduce congestion affecting over 1.5 million residents. The proposal, backed by Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde, will be sent to the National Highway Authority for approval. Relocation is expected to take 1 to 1.5 months, ahead of the Diwali festival.
headline: Maharashtra Plans to Relocate Dahisar Toll Plaza Near Versova Bridge

author: News18
summary: INS Tamal, India''s last imported stealth frigate, arrives at its Karwar base on Wednesday after a journey through multiple seas. Commissioned on July 1 in Russia, it visited ports including Morocco and Greece, engaging in various cooperative activities. The Talwar-class frigate is equipped with advanced defense systems and has a crew of 250 sailors and 26 officers.
headline: INS Tamal to Receive Grand Welcome at Karwar After Voyage from Russia

author: News18
summary: Aizawl will receive direct trains from Delhi, Kolkata, and Guwahati this week via the 34-km Hortoki–Sairang line, completing the Bairabi–Sairang project. This milestone reduces travel time between Aizawl and Silchar to three hours. The new line, featuring 32 tunnels and 35 bridges, will enhance trade and travel in the region. Initial services will include a Rajdhani train to Delhi and two trains to Kolkata and Guwahati.
headline: Aizawl Gets Direct Train Links from Delhi, Kolkata, Guwahati with New Rail Line

author: News18
summary: The Kerala State Lottery Department will announce the Karunya Plus KN 589 results on September 11, 2025, at 3 PM in Thiruvananthapuram. Cash prizes include Rs 1 crore for the first prize, Rs 30 lakh for the second, and Rs 5 lakh for the third. Winners must verify their ticket against the official results and claim their prizes within 30 days, providing the necessary documents. Check the full list of winning numbers after the draw.
headline: Kerala Lottery Result for Karunya Plus KN-589 on September 11, 2025

author: News18
summary: Twenty-two Nepali prisoners were apprehended by India’s SSB at the Indo-Nepal border after escaping during unrest. They failed to show documents when questioned. SSB heightened patrols and sealed the border due to violence in Nepal, where hundreds escaped from various jails. Specific intelligence on these prisoners was available, and multiple agencies are now questioning them. The situation is stable, but vigilance remains high on the Indian side.
headline: 22 Nepali Prisoners Caught by India's SSB After Escaping Amid Nepal Unrest

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a rape complaint filed four years after the alleged incident, calling it “frivolous and vexatious.” Justices JB Pardiwala and Sandeep Mehta noted the lack of crucial details and the complainant’s failure to respond. The court clarified that a genuine promise of marriage, if unfulfilled, does not constitute rape unless malicious intent is proven. The ruling criticized lower courts for allowing the case to proceed without a solid foundation.
headline: SC Dismisses Rape Complaint Over Promise Of Marriage, Calls It Frivolous

author: News18
summary: India announced special flights from Kathmandu to bring back stranded passengers due to unrest. Civil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu stated flights would operate for the next few days, starting this evening. Air India is also running flights from Delhi to Kathmandu to assist stranded passengers. The airport in Kathmandu resumed operations after being closed due to protests, but confusion remains as many passengers report delays and lack of clear information.
headline: India Launches Special Flights from Kathmandu Amid Unrest to Rescue Stranded Nationals

author: News18
summary: The Election Commission of India convened its third Conference of Chief Electoral Officers in New Delhi to review SIR preparations. Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar led the meeting, attended by CEOs from all States and Union Territories. Key discussions included strategies for the SIR exercise, updates on elector numbers, and improving voter inclusion. The Commission plans to announce the SIR rollout date soon.
headline: ECI Holds Third CEOs' Conference on Nationwide SIR Preparedness

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court stated that a lack of treatment success does not imply doctor negligence. Justices emphasized that medical professionals do not intentionally harm patients, and courts should not substitute their views for expert opinions. The ruling overturned an earlier compensation order against Dr. Kanwarjit Kochhar, highlighting that multiple medical boards found no negligence. The court criticized the NCDRC for exceeding its jurisdiction in its findings.
headline: SC Rules Treatment Failure Not Negligence, Sets Aside Compensation Order

author: News18
summary: US Department of Defense and Boeing officials will visit India next week to discuss P-8I aircraft procurement. Despite media reports suggesting India may pause US arms purchases, Prime Minister Modi emphasized the importance of trade negotiations. The defence ministry denied claims of halted P-8I aircraft purchases and stated that procurement processes are ongoing. The India-US defence partnership remains strong, focusing on mutual respect and shared interests.
headline: US Defence and Boeing Officials to Discuss P-8I Aircraft in India Next Week

author: News18
summary: India and the US are realigning ties, focusing on trade, security cooperation, and countering geopolitical threats.
headline: India-US Relations Normalize Amid Strategic Interests

author: News18
summary: India’s Ambassador to the US, Vinay Mohan Kwatra, praised Sergio Gor’s nomination as a sign of priority in bilateral ties. He noted Gor is one of Trump’s trusted aides, emphasizing a commitment to strengthen friendship between the two nations. Trump stated Gor will remain in his current role until confirmed by the Senate. Gor expressed gratitude for the nomination, calling it “the honor of my life.”
headline: Indian Ambassador Calls Sergio Gor's Nomination A Sign Of Importance In US-India Ties

author: News18
summary: RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat, 75, dedicated his life to societal transformation, said PM Modi. Modi stated Bhagwat''s tenure marks a transformative period in RSS''s 100-year history. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh praised Bhagwat''s contributions to India''s cultural values and his dedication to nation-building. Bhagwat, born September 11, 1950, in Maharashtra, became RSS chief in March 2009 and is the third longest-serving leader.
headline: PM Modi Honors Mohan Bhagwat's 75 Years of Dedication to Societal Transformation

author: News18
summary: A Lancet study shows Indian women have a greater increase in chronic disease death risk from 2010 to 2019. Women’s risk rose from 46.7% to 48.7%, while men’s increased from 56% to 57.9%. The report highlights the need for effective healthcare interventions to combat rising non-communicable diseases. Globally, chronic disease mortality decreased in 80% of countries, but progress has slowed, necessitating urgent healthcare policy rollouts.
headline: Indian Women Face Higher Chronic Disease Death Risk Than Men: Lancet Study

author: News18
summary: The Shillong Teer lottery, held daily at Polo Stadium, offers cash prizes and promotes archery. Winning numbers for September 11 include results for Shillong Morning Teer, Juwai Teer, Khanapara Teer, and more, with rounds throughout the day. Players select numbers predicting arrow hits, with prizes based on ticket value and accuracy. A correct guess can earn significant rewards, making it a popular game in Meghalaya.
headline: Shillong Teer Results for September 11, 2025: Winning Numbers Announced

author: News18
summary: The government has relaxed distance rules for Jan Aushadhi Kendras in metro and million-plus cities to enhance access to affordable generic medicines. No minimum distance is needed between Kendras in seven metropolitan areas, while cities with over one million residents will also follow this rule, except for Kendras under two years old. Existing rules remain for smaller towns. Compliance is mandated for processing new Kendras under the PMBJP scheme.
headline: Govt Eases Distance Norms for Jan Aushadhi Kendras in Metro and Million-Plus Cities

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court questioned the Centre''s claim of false alarms over pending bills during a hearing. A five-judge bench, led by CJI BR Gavai, addressed the issue of governors'' timelines for assessing bills. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta stated that only 20 out of 17,000 bills were withheld since 1970. The court is reviewing 14 questions from President Droupadi Murmu, including the power of authorities to delay assent to bills.
headline: Supreme Court Challenges Centre's 'False Alarm' Claim on Delayed Bills

author: News18
summary: Air India will operate special flights from Delhi to Kathmandu on Wednesday and Thursday due to unrest in Nepal. IndiGo will also resume scheduled services from September 11, adding two special relief flights. Both airlines aim to assist stranded passengers, with Air India thanking the government for coordination. Passengers are advised to check flight status and contact customer support for assistance.
headline: Air India, IndiGo Launch Special Flights to Kathmandu Amid Unrest

author: News18
summary: The Delhi High Court questioned a notification allowing police to depose via video from police stations, stating it may compromise fair trial rights. A PIL argued this gives police an unfair advantage. The court emphasized the need for neutrality during depositions, and the government promised to address concerns in the next hearing set for December 10.
headline: Delhi HC Raises Fair Trial Concerns Over LG's Video Deposition Notification

author: News18
summary: Reliance has initiated a 10-point humanitarian response in Punjab following devastating floods, providing food, water, emergency shelters, and livestock care. The plan aims to support 10,000 families with dry ration kits, sanitation essentials, and veterinary care for distressed animals. Reliance teams are coordinating with local authorities to deliver immediate assistance and ensure recovery efforts for affected communities.
headline: Reliance Launches 10-Point Humanitarian Plan for Flood-Hit Punjab

author: News18
summary: The Bombay High Court criticized Maharashtra Police for not releasing Ramesh Gaichor, granted bail on August 26 to visit his sick father. Authorities cited a public holiday for the delay. The court questioned why a release warrant was needed when bail was granted on humanitarian grounds. Public Prosecutor Mankunwar Deshmukh explained confusion over legal requirements. Gaichor has been in custody since September 2020 and hasn''t seen his father in over four years.
headline: Court Criticizes Delay in Releasing Elgar Parishad Accused Ramesh Gaichor

author: News18
summary: The Delhi High Court ruled a wife cannot be denied maintenance due to her income. The court increased her maintenance from Rs 35,000 to Rs 1,50,000 per month, citing a stark disparity with her husband''s income exceeding Rs 1.5 crore. The bench emphasized that maintenance should ensure neither spouse suffers economically after divorce, rejecting the husband''s claim that it encourages idleness.
headline: Court Raises Maintenance to Rs 1.5 Lakh for Employed Wife Amid Income Disparity

author: News18
summary: PM Modi spoke with Qatar’s Amir Sheikh Tamim, expressing concern over attacks in Doha and condemning Israel’s violation of Qatar''s sovereignty. He affirmed India''s support for peace, dialogue, and stability in the region. Modi also appreciated Qatar’s mediation efforts in Gaza. The strikes followed a Hamas shooting in Jerusalem, resulting in casualties, and prompted diplomatic responses from Gulf leaders and criticism from the EU.
headline: PM Modi Condemns Israeli Strikes, Supports Qatar''s Sovereignty

summary: The ''Thak-Thak'' gang is creating panic among vehicle owners, targeting them at night or traffic signals. They use a loud '
created_by: claiming issues with their vehicles. While drivers check
headline: accomplices steal valuables. Active in cities like Delhi and Mumbai

author: News18
summary: CMs of Delhi, Andhra Pradesh, Karnataka, and Kerala directed officials to assist citizens stranded in Nepal amid civil unrest. Protests erupted over a government ban on social media, escalating after a deadly crackdown. Karnataka''s CM Siddaramaiah is ensuring the safe return of 30 stranded Kannadigas, while Andhra''s CM Naidu reported 200 Telugu people affected. Kerala''s Vijayan requested intervention for evacuations, and Delhi''s Gupta communicated with embassies for safe returns.
headline: CMs of Andhra, Karnataka, Kerala, Delhi Act to Bring Stranded Citizens Home from Nepal

author: News18
summary: A legal dispute in Delhi High Court involves Karisma Kapoor''s children seeking a share of their late father Sunjay Kapur''s estate. They accuse stepmother Priya Kapur of manipulating the will to usurp the assets. Legal experts warn that challenging the will may be difficult without solid evidence. Sunjay passed away in June 2025, leaving behind his wife and two children, who question the authenticity of the will presented weeks after his death.
headline: Karisma Kapoor''s Kids Demand Share of Sunjay Kapur''s Rs 30,000-Crore Estate

author: News18
summary: NDA candidate CP Radhakrishnan was elected as India''s 15th Vice President, defeating B Sudershan Reddy. His mother, Janaki Ammal, named him after former President Dr Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan, recalling their shared profession as teachers. She expressed joy over her son''s victory, while his brother, CP Kumaresh, praised Radhakrishnan''s new responsibilities in managing the Rajya Sabha. President Murmu and PM Modi congratulated him on his election.
headline: CP Radhakrishnan Elected 15th Vice President of India, Mother Shares Naming Story

author: News18
summary: Ravindra Singh Chauhan, earning Rs 10,000 monthly, received a Rs 46 crore Income Tax notice. He claims his documents were misused to open a fake account linked to Shaurya Trading Company, causing a legal nightmare. Chauhan approached Gwalior High Court after failing to get authorities to act on his complaints about the misuse. He discovered the fake account after receiving the notice again, having ignored the first due to language barriers.
headline: MP Cook With Rs 10,000 Salary Receives Rs 46 Cr Tax Notice

author: News18
summary: Two cases from Ahmedabad reveal dark marriage realities: one woman accuses her husband of porn addiction and abuse, while another cites a secret affair. Both have filed complaints of domestic violence against their husbands and in-laws. The first woman, married in 2020, claims her husband, Dharmik, and his parents harassed her over dowry and belittled her gifts.
headline: Shocking Marriage Horror Tales Emerge From Ahmedabad

author: News18
summary: Congress MP Manish Tewari has called for a thorough investigation into allegations of cross-voting by the INDIA bloc MPs during the recent Vice Presidential elections, where NDA candidate CP Radhakrishnan won. Tewari labeled cross-voting as a serious betrayal of trust, urging each member of the INDIA Alliance to investigate. BJP leaders have suggested that some opposition MPs voted for Radhakrishnan, contributing to the INDIA bloc''s vote shortfall of 15.
headline: Congress's Manish Tewari Demands Probe Into Cross-Voting Claims in VP Elections

summary: At the UN Human Rights Council, Indian diplomat Kshitij Tyagi criticized Pakistan as a '
created_by: calling its leadership''s comments on the country a metaphor for its falsehoods and propaganda. This marks another strong rebuttal from Tyagi against Pakistan''s actions and claims."

author: News18
summary: In today’s digest, Indian students in Nepal fear for safety amid violence, while 200 were arrested in France during protests over the PM’s ouster. The Delhi government announced a plan for pet management ahead of World Rabies Day. The Supreme Court emphasized the Indian Constitution amid unrest in Nepal and Bangladesh. Additionally, Sunjay Kapur''s inheritance dispute raises questions about his relationship with ex-wife Karisma Kapoor.
headline: Nepal Violence Affects Indian Students; Protests in France Lead to 200 Arrests

author: News18
summary: Commuters on the Samruddhi Expressway faced punctured tyres from nails embedded in the road in Maharashtra. At least three cars were affected early Wednesday. A video shows the nails, raising safety concerns. Transporter Santosh Sanap reported hundreds of nails and claimed police did not respond promptly. Authorities are investigating the incident, summoning the road maintenance contractor for further details.
headline: Nails Puncture Tyres on Samruddhi Expressway, Suspected Repair Gang Involved

author: News18
summary: In India, rabies poses a serious threat, with the country accounting for 36% of global rabies deaths. Viral videos of victims have fueled fear. With over 30,000 deaths annually, understanding rabies, its transmission, and safety tips is crucial for protection against this deadly disease.
headline: Rabies Risk: Understanding Dog Bites and How to Stay Safe

author: News18
summary: Violence and unrest in Nepal leave Indian students anxious and confined to campuses. Binita Manna, a second-year MBBS student, reports feeling safe inside but fears for her safety outside, where incidents of arson and looting occur. Her exams are postponed, and poor mobile networks hinder communication with family. She wishes to return to India, expressing deep concern for her safety amid the chaos.
headline: Indian Students in Nepal Fear for Safety Amid Ongoing Violence

author: News18
summary: An eyewitness recounts the chaotic moments during the shooting of MAGA activist Charlie Kirk at Utah Valley University. The witness details the shock and panic among the crowd. Authorities are intensifying their search for the suspect as the investigation continues.
headline: Eyewitness Describes Charlie Kirk Shooting at Utah Valley University

author: News18
summary:
Ask a child to imagine a dream job, and many would picture themselves behind the giant steering wheel of a bus. The sight of a driver, commanding a big machine through busy streets, carried a certain charm. But fast forward to adulthood, and that dream crashes into hard reality. Today, in Kolkata and across Bengal, hardly anyone wants to be a bus driver.
A Vanishing Profession
headline: From Pride To Decline: Why The Driver’s Seat No Longer Holds Any Glory

summary: The Uttarakhand High Court issued a notice to BCCI regarding petitions on alleged misuse of ₹12 crore in CAU funds. Petitioners cited an audit report listing ₹35 lakh for '
