author: News18
summary: Karisma Kapoor’s children claim their late father Sunjay Kapur’s will is unregistered and forged. They seek a share of his estate worth Rs 30,000 crore and interim relief. Concerns were raised over the will''s sudden disclosure and the executor''s involvement. The court has ordered written statements from all parties within two weeks and directed Priya Kapur to list all estate assets. Rani Kapur expressed concern over transparency regarding the inheritance.
headline: Karisma Kapoor's Children Challenge Father's Will in Delhi High Court

author: News18
summary: Bihar Deputy CM Samrat Choudhary attributed Nepal''s unrest to the Congress party, claiming their policies isolated the country. He suggested that if Nepal were part of India, it would enjoy peace and prosperity. The political crisis in Nepal follows violent protests against the government, resulting in multiple fatalities and heightened tensions among the youth. Nepal is currently under military rule as protests continue.
headline: Bihar Deputy CM Blames Congress for Nepal's Political Unrest

author: News18
summary: A Delhi court ordered Rs 10 lakh in damages to a BSF officer after finding false sexual harassment allegations by a colleague caused reputational harm. District Judge Gunjan Gupta noted that while sexual harassment is serious, false claims violate dignity and can damage lives. An inquiry confirmed the allegations were baseless, leading to the awarded compensation. The judgment emphasizes the need to deter misuse of workplace protection laws.
headline: Court Awards Rs 10 Lakh to BSF Officer Over False Sexual Harassment Claims

author: News18
summary: Sunjay Kapur''s death in June has led to a legal feud over his ₹30,000-crore estate. His children, Samaira and Kiaan, claim a fabricated will benefits his widow, Priya Sachdev Kapur, who defends her claim citing prior assets. Rani Kapur, Sunjay''s mother, claims she''s left without a roof and contests the RK Trust''s management. The Delhi High Court will hear the case further on October 9.
headline: Sunjay Kapur''s Death Sparks Legal Battle Over ₹30,000-Crore Fortune

author: News18
summary: Namo Bharat and Meerut Metro services will soon begin operations, inaugurated by PM Modi. For the first time, a local metro will share tracks with the Namo Bharat train. Trains will run every 10 minutes, improved from 15. Final testing is underway, ensuring smooth performance. Namo Bharat will reach 160 km/h, while Meerut Metro will hit 120 km/h, both operated from a centralized Control Center.
headline: Namo Bharat, Meerut Metro Trains to Operate on Same Tracks Soon

author: News18
summary: Thippiri Tirupathi, alias Devuji, is the new CPI (Maoist) general secretary following Basava Raju''s death. He was previously chief of the Central Military Commission. Hidma, a wanted Maoist, now oversees operations in Bastar. Tirupathi, linked to the 2010 Dantewada massacre, has a reward of Rs 10 lakh on him. Union Home Minister Amit Shah aims to end Naxalism by 2026, with significant reductions in violence reported.
headline: New Maoist Chief Thippiri Tirupathi Appointed After Basava Raju's Death

author: News18
summary: A yoga teacher in Kanpur lost over Rs 8.49 lakh after falling for a WhatsApp traffic challan scam. He received a false message about a challan, entered his bank details, and lost the money. The incident occurred on September 3 when a constable photographed a broken number plate. Authorities warn citizens about increasing cyber fraud tactics and advise caution against clicking unknown links.
headline: Yoga Teacher Loses Rs 8.49 Lakh to WhatsApp Traffic Challan Scam

author: News18
summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court dissolved a 1998 marriage, noting that refusing divorce traps parties in misery. Justice Dhagat and Justice Choubey allowed husband Dilip Kumar''s appeal against a Tikamgarh court''s 2006 dismissal of his divorce petition from wife Chetna, citing cruelty and mental illness. The couple had been separated since 2003. The court emphasized that marriages beyond repair should not be sustained, allowing for justice under inherent powers.
headline: MP High Court Dissolves 1998 Marriage, Citing Prolonged Misery

author: News18
summary:
The temple town of Tirumala is no stranger to acts of devotion expressed through generous donations. Every day, the Tirumala Tirupati Devasthanams (TTD) receives contributions worth crores of rupees from devotees. These offerings fund everything from free meals to healthcare for the poor, reflecting the enduring bond between faith and service.
The Latest Offering From Bengaluru
headline: This Bengaluru Devotee Donated Over Rs 1 Crore To Tirumala Tirupati Temple

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court noted political upheaval in Nepal and Bangladesh, emphasizing pride in the Indian Constitution. CJI BR Gavai remarked on the situation in Nepal, where protests led to PM Oli''s resignation. The court discussed whether timelines for governors and the President on state assembly bills could be imposed. Meanwhile, Nepal faces escalating protests, with curfews imposed after violence, prompting UN concerns over human rights and fatalities.
headline: SC Highlights Importance of Constitution Amid Protests in Nepal, Bangladesh

author: News18
summary: VP-designate CP Radhakrishnan will take oath on September 12 at 10:05 am, administered by President Murmu. He was elected with 452 votes against 300 for B Sudershan Reddy. The voting turnout was 98.2%, with 752 valid ballots. Congratulations came from President Murmu and Prime Minister Modi. The Vice President''s post was vacant since July 21, 2025, after Jagdeep Dhankhar''s resignation. Radhakrishnan previously served as the Governor of Maharashtra.
headline: CP Radhakrishnan to be sworn in as India's 15th Vice President on September 12

author: News18
summary: Astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla, after the Axiom-4 mission, described how microgravity affects the body. He emphasized the importance of continuing work in space despite challenges. Shukla shared thrilling details of re-entry, likening the capsule''s speed to traveling from Lucknow to Delhi in 60 seconds. He highlighted the value of enjoying the journey, while also announcing AKTU''s plans for Space Excellence Centres and internships with private space companies.
headline: Astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla Shares Space Travel Experiences at AKTU

author: News18
summary: Bengaluru will face a 60-hour water supply disruption from September 15 to 17 due to emergency maintenance. BWSSB will temporarily shut down pumping stations under Cauvery Stage V and perform maintenance at other phases. BWSSB chairman V Ram Prasath Manohar emphasized the necessity of these works for the Cauvery Water Supply Project. Residents are urged to store water in advance as the shutdown affects areas reliant on this project.
headline: Bengaluru Water Supply Disruption From September 15-17

author: News18
summary: Pradeep and Kapil Negi, who married Sunita in July, are grieving their father''s death in Sirmaur, Himachal Pradesh. They shared a heartfelt tribute on social media, expressing their sorrow and recalling his guidance. Their father had battled cancer. The post received over a thousand condolences. The brothers made headlines for their unique marriage under the Jodidar tradition, which helps prevent property division.
headline: Himachal Brothers Mourn Father After Marrying Same Bride

author: News18
summary: Concerts Aren’t Just Bigger, They’re A Whole Culture Now: Anand Kanwar Of Artkonnect With More |N18S
headline: Concerts Aren’t Just Bigger, They’re A Whole Culture Now: Anand Kanwar Of Artkonnect With More |N18S

author: News18
summary: The Telangana ACB found quid pro quo in the Formula E Race case, alleging BRS gained Rs 44 crore. The probe revealed HMDA lost Rs 75.88 crore, while Formula-E Operations gained Rs 46 crore. ACB seeks approval to prosecute KTR, who denies wrongdoing, claiming no money exchanged. The case, stemming from a complaint on October 18, 2023, will proceed to the Vigilance Commission for further action.
headline: Telangana ACB Links BRS to Rs 44 Crore in Electoral Bonds in Formula E Case

author: News18
summary: A 25-year-old woman was allegedly raped at Singrauli railway station by Devlal Saket, 22. The survivor had to travel 300 km to Katni to file an FIR due to no woman officer being available. GRP Jabalpur station in-charge Sanjeevani Rajput later went to Katni to record her statement. The couple, from Sonbhadra, had arrived in Singrauli to catch a train when the incident occurred past midnight on September 6. The investigation is ongoing.
headline: Rape Survivor Travels 300 Km to File FIR Due to Lack of Woman Officer

author: News18
summary: ISRO and IN-SPACe signed the 100th tech transfer agreement with HAL for Small Satellite Launch Vehicle production. This aims to boost the private space economy and meet satellite launch demands. The transfer process will take 24 months, with ISRO supporting HAL in mastering SSLV technology. HAL plans to manufacture 6-12 rockets annually from 2027, starting with two prototypes. This marks the first complete launch vehicle tech transfer from a space agency to a company.
headline: ISRO, IN-SPACe Sign 100th Tech Transfer for SSLV Production with HAL

author: News18
summary: Cyber fraudsters are leveraging AI tools to target not just ordinary citizens but also political leaders, film stars, and judges. This rise in sophisticated scams highlights the darker side of technological advancements.
headline: Cyber Fraudsters Use AI to Target Karnataka Governor

author: Deepak Verma
summary:
नई दिल्ली: प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी ने नेपाल में जारी जेन-जी प्रदर्शनों और हिंसा पर गहरी चिंता जताई. हिमाचल प्रदेश और पंजाब से लौटने के बाद उन्होंने कैबिनेट कमेटी ऑन सिक्योरिटी की बैठक में नेपाल की स्थिति पर चर्चा की. मोदी ने एक्स पर नेपाली भाषा में भी संदेश साझा किया. उन्होंने लिखा कि नेपाल में हिंसा हृदयविदारक है और कई युवाओं की जानें गईं, जिससे वह बेहद व्यथित हैं. उन्होंने कहा, ‘नेपाल की स्थिरता, शांति और समृद्धि भारत के लिए अत्यंत महत्वपूर्ण है.’ पीएम मोदी ने नेपाल के सभी नागरिकों से अपील की कि वे संयम और शांति बनाए रखें. उन्होंने आश्वासन दिया कि भारत हालात पर नजदीकी नजर बनाए हुए है और नेपाल के लोगों के साथ है.

आज दिनभरीको भ्रमणबाट फर्किएपछि सुरक्षा सम्बन्धी मन्त्रिपरिषद् समितिको बैठकमा नेपालको घटनाक्रमहरुको बारेमा विस्तृत छलफल भयो । नेपालमा भएको हिंसा हृदयविदारक छ । धेरै युवाहरुले आफ्नो ज्यान गुमाउनु परेकोमा मेरो मन अत्यन्तै विचलित छ । नेपालको स्थिरता, शान्ति र समृद्धि अत्यन्त…
— Narendra Modi (@narendramodi) September 9, 2025

नेपाल में गहराता संकट: ओली के इस्तीफे के बाद भी नहीं थमी जेन-जी की आग
काठमांडू मंगलवार को सियासी और सामाजिक हलचलों के केंद्र में तब्दील हो गया. प्रधानमंत्री केपी शर्मा ओली ने इस्तीफा दे दिया, लेकिन हालात शांत होने के बजाय और भड़क उठे. प्रदर्शनकारियों ने संसद, अदालतों, नेताओं के आवास और दफ्तरों तक को आग के हवाले कर दिया.
सोशल मीडिया बैन से शुरू हुआ जेन-जी का आंदोलन अब भ्रष्टाचार, असमानता और राजनीतिक घमंड के खिलाफ बड़ी लड़ाई में बदल गया है. पुलिस कार्रवाई में 19 प्रदर्शनकारियों की मौत ने आक्रोश को और भड़का दिया. भीड़ ने ओली के निजी घर से लेकर पूर्व प्रधानमंत्री झलनाथ खनाल और शेरबहादुर देउबा तक के घरों पर हमला कर दिया. खनाल की पत्नी गंभीर रूप से झुलस गईं और बाद में उनकी मौत हो गई.
नेपाल में सेना को देना पड़ा दखल
काठमांडू और ललितपुर के कई इलाकों में टायर जलाकर सड़कें जाम कर दी गईं. अदालतों में घुसकर जरूरी फाइलें जला दी गईं. यहां तक कि पूर्व गृह मंत्री रमेश लेखक और संचार मंत्री पृथ्वी सुब्बा गुरुंग के घरों को भी नुकसान पहुंचाया गया.
ओली ने राष्ट्रपति रामचंद्र पौडेल को भेजे पत्र में ‘असाधारण परिस्थितियों’ का हवाला देते हुए पद छोड़ा. राष्ट्रपति ने अपील की, ‘देश कठिन दौर से गुजर रहा है. मैं सभी नागरिकों और प्रदर्शनकारियों से शांति और एकजुटता की अपील करता हूं.’
नेपाल आर्मी और सुरक्षा एजेंसियों ने भी संयुक्त बयान जारी कर संयम और बातचीत से हल खोजने की बात कही. आर्मी चीफ अशोक राज सिगदेल सहित शीर्ष अधिकारियों ने कहा कि अब और जान-माल का नुकसान नहीं होना चाहिए.
headline: PM मोदी का नेपाल को सीधा मैसेज- आपकी शांति हमारे लिए जरूरी, हालात पर नजर है

author: News18
summary: A Thane court acquitted a 54-year-old man accused of molesting a three-year-old girl, ruling that kissing her on the cheek lacked criminal intent. Special Judge Ruby U Malvankar stated the prosecution failed to prove charges under the POCSO Act and IPC. The court noted gestures of affection are not offensive unless intent to harm is evident, and highlighted lapses in the prosecution''s case, including unreliable witness testimonies.
headline: Thane Court Acquits Man in 2021 Molestation Case Due to Lack of Criminal Intent

author: News18
summary: Ahead of World Rabies Day on September 28, Delhi''s government announced a plan for pet and stray management. It mandates registration for pet shops and microchipping for 10 lakh dogs over two years with UNDP support. The plan includes digital vaccination records, expanded gaushalas, and organized stray feeding. Noncompliance will incur a Rs 3,000 penalty, and pet licensing requires proof of microchip and anti-rabies vaccination.
headline: Delhi Launches Action Plan for Pet and Stray Dog Management Ahead of World Rabies Day

author: News18
summary: The IMD forecasts heavy to very heavy rains across parts of India for the next five days. Northeastern states, including Arunachal Pradesh and Assam, will see significant rainfall from September 12-15. North-West India, including Uttar Pradesh and Jammu & Kashmir, is also expected to experience heavy rains. South India will see showers in Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and Andhra Pradesh. Residents are advised to stay vigilant due to risks of landslides and waterlogging.
headline: IMD Warns of Heavy Rains Across India Until September 15

author: News18
summary: New filings in the Sunjay Kapur inheritance case reveal he was helping Karisma Kapoor and their children obtain Portuguese citizenship. Chats suggest ongoing personal discussions between the ex-spouses. The Delhi High Court is examining these documents as part of a civil suit filed by their children for a share in Kapur''s ₹30,000 crore estate. The court has issued notices and scheduled the next hearing for October 9.
headline: Sunjay Kapur Aids Ex-Wife Karisma Kapoor in Securing Portuguese Citizenship for Children

author: News18
summary: Today''s News18 digest covers Nepal''s Gen-Z protests, Sunjay Kapur''s inheritance dispute, Prithvi Shaw fined in a molestation case, and Poland reporting drone breaches amid Ukraine strikes. Protests in Nepal call for a civilian-led government, while Modi emphasizes India''s close ties with the US. Two suspected ISIS terrorists were arrested in Delhi and Ranchi during a joint operation.
headline: Protests in Nepal, Inheritance Dispute & More in News18 Digest

author: News18
summary: Tensions along the India-Nepal border have escalated as protests in Nepal led to the closure of crossings in Uttar Pradesh. Security forces sealed borders, blocking civilian movement, and diverting flights. Nearly 200 trucks are stranded, and transport services have been significantly reduced. The unrest has affected daily lives, with residents feeling cut off from family. Authorities are monitoring the situation closely, prioritizing security along the 600-km border.
headline: UP on High Alert as Nepal Riots Cause Border Shutdown and Flight Diversions

author: News18
summary: Two suspected ISIS terrorists were arrested in Delhi and Ranchi on Wednesday during a joint operation by the Delhi Police Special Cell and Jharkhand ATS. Azhar Danish was arrested in Ranchi, while the other suspect was apprehended in Delhi. Ongoing raids are taking place at eight locations, including a hostel in Ranchi. The operation aims to detain individuals linked to terrorist organizations, according to police officials.
headline: 2 Suspected ISIS Terrorists Arrested in Delhi, Ranchi Raids

author: News18
summary: The family of Sunjay Kapur, who died on June 12, is in a legal battle over his Rs 30,000 crore estate. Karisma Kapoor''s children question the validity of an unregistered will. Registration is optional under the Registration Act, 1908, but provides stronger evidence. The Delhi High Court directed all parties to submit written statements within two weeks amid claims of a forged will.
headline: Sunjay Kapur's Will Dispute: Legality of Unregistered Will Under Scrutiny

author: News18
summary: Noida Police arrested three, including Raja Mian, for allegedly forcing a woman to convert and marry. The case arose after her mother sought help from the Allahabad High Court. Police traced the woman, a mother of one, to Chennai. Investigators found she was coerced into conversion and a forged nikah while still married. An FIR was filed against five, with three arrested and two suspects still at large.
headline: Noida Police Arrest Three in Forced Conversion Case

author: News18
summary: A businessman from Maharashtra, Abhijit Bhosle, was hospitalized after sipping a soft drink on a SpiceJet flight from Goa. The drink allegedly contained metal shards, causing throat irritation and stomach pain. His friends discovered the shards upon inspection. SpiceJet confirmed the incident, stating all drinks are sealed and the can was retained for investigation. Medical expenses were covered by the airline, and Bhosle declined an endoscopy after X-rays showed no foreign objects.
headline: Bizman Hospitalised After Drinking Soft Drink With Metal Shards on SpiceJet Flight

author: News18
summary: The Delhi High Court directed Priya Sachdev Kapur to list her late husband Sunjay Kapur’s assets. Karisma Kapoor’s children allege Priya forged their father’s will for control over his Rs 30,000-crore fortune. They seek a fifth of the estate, claiming they are not beneficiaries under the will. The court will hear the case again on October 9, giving two weeks for a response. Priya noted that the children received 1900 crores from the trust.
headline: Delhi HC Orders Priya Sachdev to List Sunjay Kapur's Assets Amid Inheritance Dispute

author: News18
summary: | to probe on Cross voting..(Sources)Cross-voting stings INDIA bloc as CP Radhakrishnan sweeps polls n18oc_indian18oc_politicsNews18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube
headline: Congress To Probe On Cross Voting |Cross-voting stings INDIA bloc as CP Radhakrishnan sweeps polls

author: News18
summary: A 69-year-old Mumbai woman was scammed of Rs 65,000 by a fraudster posing as Keanu Reeves, police said. The fraudster befriended her on Instagram, claiming he needed money for a flight to India. The victim''s daughter discovered the transaction after receiving a bank notification and filed a complaint. Police are investigating the case under identity theft and cheating laws, while gathering information on the accused who used a fake mobile number.
headline: Keanu Reeves Impersonator Scams Mumbai Woman of Rs 65,000 on Instagram

author: News18
summary: A 44-year-old woman, Madhavi, died after falling off her scooter due to a pothole on NH 66 near Kulur. The lorry, driven by Mohammed Farooq, ran over her at 8:30 am. Police charged Farooq with negligence and rash driving, and NHAI officials for highway maintenance negligence. This marks the third pothole-related death in the area recently, prompting criticism of NHAI for ignoring repair warnings.
headline: Woman Killed by Lorry After Falling from Scooter in Mangaluru Pothole Incident

author: Vipul Solanki
summary: બનાસકાંઠાના માંગરોળ ગામમાં 48 કલાકથી વીજળી ગુલ છે, અને વરસાદ બંધ થયા બાદ પણ પાણી ભરાયેલું છે. સ્થાનિકો કહે છે કે, ભારતમાલા રોડ પર નાળાં ન હોવાના કારણે પાણી જમા થઈ રહ્યું છે. મંદિરે દર્શન કરવા જતી શ્રદ્ધાળુઓને પણ મુશ્કેલીઓનો સામનો કરવો પડી રહ્યો છે. ખેડૂતો પાકની નુકશાની અંગે સર્વે કરવાની માંગ કરી રહ્યા છે.
headline: માંગરોળ ગામમાં 48 કલાકથી વીજળી ગુલ, વરસાદ બંધ છતાં જળબંબાકાર સ્થિતિ યથાવત

author: Nirali Gohil
summary: નવરાત્રિના આગમનથી રાજકોટમાં ચણિયાચોળીની ખરીદી શરૂ થઈ છે. 14 મીટર ઘેરવાળા ચણિયાચોળી યુવતીઓમાં લોકપ્રિય છે. 2 થી 5 હજાર રૂપિયાની રેન્જમાં માંગ છે, જ્યારે 32 થી 60 મીટર ઘેરવાળા ચણિયાચોળી ટ્રેન્ડમાં છે. ફેશન ઉદ્યોગકારોનો અંદાજ છે કે આ વર્ષે વેપાર 60 કરોડ સુધી પહોંચી શકે છે. યુવતીઓ પરંપરાગત અને આધુનિક ડિઝાઈનને અપનાવી રહી છે.
headline: રાજકોટમાં નવરાત્રિના તહેવારમાં ચણિયાચોળીની ધૂમ, 60 મીટર ઘેરવાળા લોકપ્રિય

author: News18
summary: Mohd Afzal, 45, escaped police custody in Bulandshahr after pushing a constable on September 4. Over 30 villagers, including the gram pradhan, were booked for aiding his escape. Afzal had been evading arrest since a rape case was registered against him on August 28. Police are investigating the incident and have formed teams to trace him, while some villagers claim confusion led to the escape.
headline: Rape Accused Escapes Custody in Bulandshahr; 30 Villagers Booked for Help

author: News18
summary: A techie and his wife were assaulted at a Chinchwad INOX theatre after a dispute over movie spoilers. During a screening of The Conjuring: Last Rites, a man behind them recounted scenes, leading to a confrontation. The assailant attacked the techie, and his wife was also assaulted when she intervened. They reported the incident to police, who have charged the accused under multiple sections and arrested them.
headline: Pune Techie, Wife Assaulted Over Movie Spoilers at INOX Chinchwad

author: News18
summary: In the inheritance dispute over Sunjay Kapur''s estate, Priya Sachdev Kapur''s lawyer criticized Karisma Kapoor for being “nowhere to be seen for the last 15 years.” He argued that the plaintiffs already received ₹1,900 crore from the trust. The court ordered written statements in two weeks and instructed Priya to list her assets. Karisma''s children seek a share of the ₹30,000-crore estate, alleging Priya forged the will to gain control.
headline: Lawyer Claims Karisma Kapoor Absent for 15 Years in Sunjay Kapur Inheritance Case

author: News18
summary: Assam''s cabinet approved a new SOP allowing district officials to expel suspected foreigners without tribunal review. Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma stated that illegal immigrants must prove citizenship within ten days, or face expulsion on the tenth day if evidence is deemed unsatisfactory.
headline: Assam Cabinet Approves SOP for Expulsion of Suspected Foreigners

author: News18
summary: Fresh embankment breaches in Punjab''s border districts raise fears of worsening floods. Farmers blame dam mismanagement and sand mining for the crisis, calling it a ''man-made disaster.'' They demand full compensation of Rs 70,000 per acre for crop losses instead of the Rs 20,000 offered. The IMD confirmed this August was the wettest since 2001, with floods impacting over 2,000 villages and killing at least 40 people. Rescue operations continue as experts call for transparency in dam operations.
headline: Punjab Floods: Farmers Demand Accountability Amid Fresh Embankment Breaches

author: News18
summary: CCB arrested constable Prashant Navi and Nasir Khan for extorting money from BBMP waste collectors. Khan, posing as a police officer, threatened victims and kidnapped some to demand money. The racket surfaced during a health camp in Varthur after victims reported their experiences. Navi, previously in ISD, was suspended and confessed to his involvement after his arrest on September 8. Both are now in judicial custody.
headline: Constable and Accomplice Arrested for Extorting Bengaluru Waste Collectors

author: News18
summary: PM Modi and President Trump may soon speak by phone, potentially easing tensions since their last call on June 17. Both leaders expressed eagerness to connect, with discussions on trade and upcoming summits. A successful call could lead to a meeting, possibly in Malaysia during the ASEAN summit in October. However, challenges remain, including unresolved tariffs and differing approaches to India-Pakistan relations.
headline: Modi and Trump to Discuss Relations Over Phone Soon

author: News18
summary: PM Modi labeled the US a “close friend” and “natural partner,” expressing confidence in trade talks. He noted progress in negotiations to unlock the India-US partnership''s potential. Trump echoed optimism, stating he anticipates a successful trade conclusion, despite recent tariff increases on Indian goods.
headline: Modi Calls US '

author: News18
summary: Three soldiers, including two Agniveers, died in an avalanche at Siachen base camp in Ladakh. A rescue operation was launched, and the bodies were retrieved on Tuesday.
headline: Avalanche Kills Three Soldiers in Siachen, World''s Highest Battlefield

author: News18
summary: Nepal''s protests against censorship and corruption have left 19 dead, affecting nearly 200,000 Indians. Indian-owned businesses in Kathmandu and Pokhara face significant losses due to violence. Nearly 2,500 Indian tourists are stranded at Tribhuvan International Airport, with curfews complicating evacuations. Online hostility fueled by misinformation further endangers Indians, prompting India''s MEA to issue a travel advisory and emergency assistance. The situation remains tense and volatile.
headline: Indians Face Crisis in Nepal Amid Protests, Stranded Tourists, and Rising Hostility

author: News18
summary: Araria’s Jogbani will debut on Vande Bharat and Amrit Bharat maps with two new trains starting September 15. The Vande Bharat train will connect Danapur in Patna, while the Amrit Bharat train will link Erode in Tamil Nadu. Bihar will have a total of 10 Amrit Bharat trains operational, enhancing connectivity, especially for commuters to Nepal. This expansion boosts Bihar’s Vande Bharat count to 14, improving rail travel efficiency and convenience.
headline: Jogbani to Feature on Vande Bharat, Amrit Bharat Maps from September 15

author: News18
summary: Indian pilgrims returning from Kailash Mansarovar are stranded at Kathmandu''s airport for over 24 hours due to protests. Four of six flights were canceled after the airport closed Tuesday at 1 p.m. Nearly 700 travelers, including 130 from Isha Foundation, are stuck without food or water. Chaos ensued as airport staff were ordered to leave. Protests followed the resignation of Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli after violent clashes, resulting in 19 deaths and injuries.
headline: Indian Pilgrims Stranded at Nepal Airport Amid Protests for Over 24 Hours

author: News18
summary: A woman accidentally drove a Thar through a Mahindra showroom in East Delhi after buying it. While performing a pooja, she pressed the accelerator, causing the vehicle to smash through a glass wall. No injuries were reported, and both she and a passenger were treated at a nearby hospital. The woman, Maani Pawar, intended to crush a lemon under the car’s tyre as part of the ritual. No complaint was filed following the incident.
headline: Woman Crashes SUV Through Mahindra Showroom After Purchase in Delhi

author: News18
summary: A 48-year-old cab driver, Lom Shankar, was arrested for allegedly masturbating in front of a Delhi University student. The incident occurred on Monday as the student traveled from Model Town. After refusing to sit in the front seat, the driver made lewd remarks and assaulted her. She escaped at North Campus and reported the incident. Police seized the vehicle for forensic analysis and are providing the student with counseling.
headline: Delhi Cab Driver Arrested for Indecent Act in Front of DU Student

author: News18
summary: The US Senate introduced the HIRE Act, proposing a 25% tax on outsourcing payments to foreign firms. This aims to discourage US companies from sending jobs abroad, with funds supporting domestic training. If passed, it would apply to payments after December 31, 2025. Indian IT firms, reliant on US clients for revenues, face potential cost increases and must adapt amid rising automation and economic pressures.
headline: US HIRE Act Proposes 25% Tax on Outsourcing, Impacting Indian IT Sector

author: News18
summary: Kerala Lottery results for September 10, 2025, are out. The 1st prize of ₹1 crore went to ticket DD 781756, sold in Thrissur. The 2nd prize of ₹30 lakh was won by ticket DC 594113 in Kannur, and the 3rd prize of ₹5 lakh by ticket DJ 508838 in Kattappana. The draw was held at Gorky Bhavan, Thiruvananthapuram. Check the full list of winning numbers below.
headline: Kerala Lottery Result 10-09-2025: DL-17 Bumper Draw Declared – ₹1 Crore Winner Announced

author: News18
summary: Former Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar congratulated CP Radhakrishnan, the new Vice President, on Tuesday. Dhankhar stated Radhakrishnan''s experience would enhance the office''s glory. Radhakrishnan won the election with 452 votes against 300 for the Opposition candidate. Dhankhar resigned in July, citing health issues, prompting the election.
headline: Dhankhar Congratulates Successor Radhakrishnan in First Statement Since Exit

author: News18
summary: Shillong Teer lottery results for September 10, 2025, are live. The game, held at Polo Stadium, promotes archery in Meghalaya. Winning numbers include Shillong Morning Teer, Juwai Teer, Khanapara Teer, and Night Teer. The lottery consists of two rounds where players bet on numbers predicting arrow strikes. Correct guesses can yield substantial cash prizes, with potential earnings up to Rs 4,000 for a Rs 1 ticket.
headline: Shillong Teer Results for September 10, 2025: Winning Numbers Announced

author: News18
summary: The Assam cabinet approved a new SOP allowing district commissioners to expel suspected foreigners without tribunal referral. Illegal immigrants will have ten days to prove citizenship, or face expulsion. Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma stated this speeds up the process. The SOP does not affect the 82,000 pending tribunal cases. Additional measures included financial aid for flood-hit Himachal Pradesh and pay corrections for staff at Jorhat Medical College.
headline: Assam Cabinet Approves SOP for Expulsion of Illegal Immigrants Without Tribunal

author: News18
summary: In 2025, Delhi''s transport department logged over 5.95 lakh pollution challans, averaging 2,500 daily. Violations of pollution control guidelines surged recently. The government is implementing a winter air pollution control action plan, discussing measures like dust control and mist sprayers. Sirsa emphasized strengthening response systems and accelerating road repairs. Agencies must meet targets before October as air quality worsens in winter months.
headline: Delhi Logs 2,500 Pollution Challans Daily in 2025, Highest in 5 Years

author: News18
summary: PM Modi chaired a Cabinet meeting on Nepal''s crisis after violent protests led to 19 deaths. He called the situation '
created_by: appealing for support from its citizens. The protests erupted over issues like a social media ban and corruption
headline: leading to the resignation of PM KP Sharma Oli and President Ram Chandra Poudel."
modified_by: PM Modi Calls for Stability Amid Nepal's Political Crisis

author: News18
summary: The CDSCO proposes Braille labels and QR codes for eye drops and other drugs to aid visually impaired patients. Public comments are invited on this initiative, aimed at improving drug accessibility. The move addresses concerns about visually impaired individuals struggling to identify medications and expiry dates. The proposal includes validation of Braille by authorized agencies and suggests accessible formats for detailed information. Industry response remains cautious regarding implementation costs.
headline: Eye Drops, Other Drugs May Get Braille Labels and QR Codes for Visually Impaired

author: Dhiraj Rai
summary: नेपाल के PM केपी शर्मा ओली का राजनीतिक सफर 14 साल की उम्र में शुरू हुआ। 14 साल जेल में बिताने के बाद, उन्होंने 1991 में सांसद बने। हाल ही में, ओली ने सोशल मीडिया बैन और विद्रोह के चलते देश छोड़ने की खबरें आ रही हैं। 2025 में Gen Z के बड़े विरोध प्रदर्शन हुए, जिसके बाद सरकार को बैन हटाना पड़ा। ओली की सरकार पर भ्रष्टाचार और कमजोर अर्थव्यवस्था के आरोप लगे हैं।
headline: केपी शर्मा ओली: नेपाल के PM और विद्रोह की वजह

author: vinoy jha
summary:
DDA Jan Sadharan Awaas Yojana 2025: दिल्‍ली में आप घर खरीदने के बारे में सोच रहे हैं तो अब आपका यह सपना पूरा हो सकता है. दरअसल, दिल्ली डेवलपमेंट अथॉरिटी यानी डीडीए (DDA) की ओर से ‘डीडीए जन साधारण आवास योजना 2025’ लाई गई है. इस योजना के लिए रजिस्ट्रेशन कल यानी 11 सितंबर से शुरू होगा.
यह योजना दिल्ली में आम लोगों को किफायती फ्लैट्स उपलब्ध कराने के उद्देश्य से शुरू की गई है. इस मौके का फायदा उठाने के लिए इच्छुक आवेदक डीडीए के आधिकारिक पोर्टल पर जाकर 11 सितंबर से रजिस्ट्रेशन कर सकते हैं. रजिस्ट्रेशन पूरा होने के बाद 22 सितंबर से फ्लैट बुकिंग की प्रक्रिया शुरू हो जाएगी. योजना की आखिरी तारीख 21 दिसंबर, 2025 तय की गई है.
EWS/जनता फ्लैट की बुकिंग राशि 50,000 रुपये
योजना के तहत आवंटन पहले आओ, पहले पाओ (First Come, First Serve) के आधार पर होगी. आधार पर किया जाएगा. इसके लिए रजिस्ट्रेशन चार्ज 2,500 रुपये रखा गया है. पहले से पोर्टल पर रजिस्टर्ड आवेदकों को यह चार्ज नहीं देना होगा. ईडब्ल्यूएस/जनता फ्लैट की बुकिंग राशि 50,000 रुपये होगी. परिवार की एनुअल इनकम 10 लाख रुपये से ज्यादा नहीं होनी चाहिए. आवेदक की न्यूनतम आयु 18 साल होना जरूरी है.
कितने और कहां मिलेंगे फ्लैट?
योजना के तहत ईडब्ल्यूएस (EWS) और जनता (Janta) कैटेगरी के फ्लैट्स  उपलब्ध होंगे. ये फ्लैट दिल्ली के अलग-अलग इलाकों में उपलब्ध होंगे. इस योजना में EWS और जनता कैटेगरी के कुल 1172 फ्लैट्स शामिल हैं.
नरेला (EWS): 672 फ्लैट्स, कीमत- 9.18 लाख रुपये से 27.86 लाख रुपये तक
लोकेनायकपुरम (EWS): 108 फ्लैट्स, कीमत- 29.60 लाख रुपये से 32.62 लाख रुपये तक
रोहिणी (Janta): 97 फ्लैट्स, कीमत- 14.59 लाख रुपये से 15 लाख रुपये तक
टोडापुर (Janta): 3 फ्लैट्स, कीमत 18.02 लाख रुपये से 18.43 लाख रुपये तक
द्वारका सेक्टर 14 (EWS): 241 फ्लैट्स, कीमत 34.74 लाख रुपये से 35.32 लाख रुपये तक
द्वारका सेक्टर 19B (EWS): 3 फ्लैट्स, कीमत 26.77 लाख रुपये
द्वारका मंगला पुरी (EWS): 48 फ्लैट्स, कीमत 32.32 लाख रुपये से 33.43 लाख रुपये तक
आवेदन कैसे करें?
रजिस्ट्रेशन और बुकिंग की पूरी प्रक्रिया DDA के आवास पोर्टल eservices.dda.org.in पर होगी. डिटेल ब्रॉशर जल्द ही www.dda.gov.in पर उपलब्ध कराया जाएगा.
योजना की खास बातें

रजिस्ट्रेशन शुरू: 11 सितंबर 2025
बुकिंग की शुरुआत: 22 सितंबर 2025
योजना का समापन: 21 दिसंबर 2025
आवंटन प्रक्रिया: पहले आओ, पहले पाओ के आधार पर होगी.
headline: दिल्‍ली में खरीदना चाहते हैं घर तो सुनहरा मौका, कल से शुरू होगा रजिस्ट्रेशन

author: Jai Thakur
summary: डेटा सेंटर से जुड़े स्टॉक्स पर निवेशकों की नजर है। Avener Capital की रिपोर्ट के अनुसार, 2024 में इसकी क्षमता 1.3 गीगावॉट होगी, जो 2030 तक 5 गीगावॉट तक पहुंच सकती है।
headline: 5 फ्यूचर प्रूफ स्टॉक्स: AI के वर्चस्व में कमाई का मौका

author: vandana
summary: काठमांडू का ''दिल्लीबाजार'' नाम भारत-नेपाल के पुराने व्यापार संबंधों से जुड़ा है। यहाँ दिल्ली और उत्तरी भारत के व्यापारी व्यापार करते थे। यह क्षेत्र व्यापारिक और सांस्कृतिक मेलजोल का प्रतीक है, जो आज भी भारतीय बाजारों की तरह व्यस्त रहता है।
headline: काठमांडू का 'दिल्लीबाजार': व्यापारिक और सांस्कृतिक इतिहास

author: Jai Thakur
summary:
नई दिल्ली. गुरुग्राम का सोहना रोड अब एनसीआर का सबसे हॉट रियल एस्टेट डेस्टिनेशन बन गया है. पिछले तीन सालों में यहां प्रॉपर्टी की कीमतों और किराए दोनों में जबरदस्त तेजी देखी गई है. एनारॉक रिसर्च की रिपोर्ट बताती है कि 2021 के आखिर से लेकर 2025 की दूसरी तिमाही तक सोहना रोड पर घरों की कीमतें 74% तक बढ़ चुकी हैं. वहीं, 2 बीएचके फ्लैट का औसत किराया भी करीब 50% बढ़कर 37,500 रुपये प्रति माह तक पहुंच गया है. यह ट्रेंड साफ दिखाता है कि सोहना रोड अब सिर्फ रहने की जगह नहीं रहा, बल्कि निवेश के लिहाज से भी बेहतरीन विकल्प बन चुका है.
एम3एम इंडिया के प्रेसिडेंट रॉबिन मंगला (Robin Mangla) कहते हैं, “सोहना रोड पर 74% की कीमतों की उछाल बताती है कि यह इलाका लक्जरी रियल एस्टेट के लिए कितना लोकप्रिय हो चुका है. मजबूत कनेक्टिविटी, कॉर्पोरेट हब के करीब लोकेशन और बेहतरीन इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर ने इसे समझदार खरीदारों के लिए प्रीमियम विकल्प बना दिया है. यहां के खरीदार अब सिर्फ घर नहीं, बल्कि लाइफस्टाइल खरीद रहे हैं.”
ये भी पढ़ें- 2 महीने से लगातार अपर सर्किट में फंस रहा ये स्टॉक, इस साल 500% पर चढ़ा, क्या अब भी कमाई का मौका?



कनेक्टिविटी और इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर बना गेम चेंजर
इंडस्ट्री एक्सपर्ट्स मानते हैं कि सोहना रोड का ग्रोथ स्टोरी इंफ्रास्ट्रक्चर पर टिकी है. दिल्ली-मुंबई एक्सप्रेसवे, सोहना एलीवेटेड रोड, गोल्फ कोर्स एक्सटेंशन रोड और एसपीआर (सदर्न पेरिफेरल रोड) ने इसकी अहमियत कई गुना बढ़ा दी है. त्रेहान आइरिस के वाइस प्रेसिडेंट अम्लान दत्ता (Amlan Dutta) कहते हैं, “2022 से अब तक यहां की प्रॉपर्टी में 74% का उछाल आया है. कॉर्पोरेट हब के नजदीक होने से किराये की मांग भी तेजी से बढ़ रही है. सोहना रोड अब एक किफायती मार्केट से प्रीमियम माइक्रो-मार्केट में बदल चुका है, जहां हाई-राइज टावर्स, लग्जरी अपार्टमेंट्स और गेटेड कम्युनिटीज खरीदारों को आकर्षित कर रही हैं.”
लक्जरी और लाइफस्टाइल की मांग
यहां नए प्रोजेक्ट्स में बड़े क्लबहाउस, हरे-भरे गार्डन और आधुनिक डिजाइन शामिल हैं. खरीदार ऐसे पते चाहते हैं जहां काम और जीवन का संतुलन बना रहे. यही वजह है कि शॉपिंग सेंटर, बिजनेस कॉम्प्लेक्स और हाई-स्ट्रीट रिटेल भी यहां तेजी से खड़े हो रहे हैं. अराइज ग्रुप की डायरेक्टर डॉ. रेणु सिंह (Dr. Renu Singh) बताती हैं, “आज के खरीदार सिर्फ चार दीवारें नहीं चाहते, बल्कि ऐसी लाइफस्टाइल चाहते हैं जो उनकी पहचान को दर्शाए. सोहना रोड लक्जरी और प्रीमियम हाउसिंग का नया केंद्र बन गया है, जहां भविष्य में कीमतें और ऊपर जाने की पूरी संभावना है.”
ये भी पढ़ें- नेपाल का इकलौता अरबपति, भारत के हर बच्चे की जुबान पर इनके नूडल्स का नाम, कितनी है दौलत?



निवेशकों और खरीदारों दोनों का फेवरेट
एनसीआर के कई इलाकों में कीमतें बढ़ी हैं, लेकिन सोहना रोड पर तेजी सबसे ज्यादा रही है. यहां का किराया भी अच्छा रिटर्न दे रहा है. यही वजह है कि यह गुरुग्राम के सबसे पसंदीदा इलाकों में शामिल हो गया है. निवेशकों को पूंजीगत लाभ और स्थिर किराये की आय दोनों का फायदा मिल रहा है, जबकि एंड-यूजर्स को मिल रही है शानदार कनेक्टिविटी और लग्जरी लाइफस्टाइल.
headline: प्रॉपर्टी खरीदने वालों के लिए चुंबक बना ये गुरुग्राम का ये इलाका!

author: News18
summary: Union Minister Jayant Chaudhary and spouse Charu Singh declared Rs 43 lakh in cryptocurrency. Chaudhary disclosed Rs 21.31 lakh and Singh Rs 22.41 lakh, citing “personal savings.” This marks the first time a Union cabinet minister has included digital assets in official filings. Their investments are part of the annual “Assets and Liabilities of the Union Council of Ministers.” Chaudhary''s total declared assets amount to Rs 33.23 crore.
headline: Jayant Chaudhary Declares Rs 43 Lakh In Crypto Assets

author: News18
summary: The ECI, following a Supreme Court order, has added Aadhaar as the 12th identity proof for Bihar''s SIR. It must be implemented strictly, with non-compliance treated seriously. The court emphasized Aadhaar is not proof of citizenship but can establish identity under the Representation of the People Act. The SIR drive ended on September 1, but new applications will be accepted until the nomination deadline.
headline: ECI Accepts Aadhaar as 12th ID Proof for Bihar's Electoral Rolls

author: News18
summary: Nepal faces a political crisis after two days of anti-government protests. Border states Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Bihar, and West Bengal are on high alert. A 24×7 control room has been set up in Lucknow to assist stranded Indian citizens. Enhanced patrolling and surveillance are in place along the Indo-Nepal border. At least 19 people died in police action during protests in Nepal, reflecting public anger over various issues, including a social media ban.
headline: Border States on High Alert Amid Nepal's Anti-Government Protests

author: News18
summary: NDA candidate CP Radhakrishnan won the Vice Presidential election, defeating B Sudershan Reddy by 152 votes. His brother, CP Kumaresh, expressed joy over the victory, stating Radhakrishnan will manage the Rajya Sabha effectively. A total of 452 lawmakers voted for Radhakrishnan, while 300 votes went to Reddy. The Vice President''s seat was vacant since July 21, 2025, following Jagdeep Dhankhar''s resignation.
headline: CP Radhakrishnan Elected 15th Vice President of India

author: News18
summary: The Telangana ACB submitted a report seeking prosecution against K.T. Rama Rao and Arvind Kumar in the Formula E race case. KTR dismissed the allegations as fabricated, asserting no money was exchanged. The case, filed on December 18, 2024, involves claims of illegal payments causing a ₹54.88 crore loss. ACB booked charges under the Prevention of Corruption Act and IPC, which could lead to life imprisonment if proven.
headline: ACB Reports on Formula E Case; KTR Calls Allegations Fabricated

author: News18
summary: CP Radhakrishnan won the Vice President election with 452 votes, defeating B Sudershan Reddy. President Droupadi Murmu and PM Modi congratulated him, expressing confidence in his leadership. Radhakrishnan will take the oath on September 12.
headline: CP Radhakrishnan Elected as India's 15th Vice President

author: News18
summary: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust reported Rs 1,400 crore spent on the Ram temple. Chairman Nripendra Mishra stated no new work will start; focus is on approved projects. An International Ram Katha Museum is underway, costing Rs 200 crore. Work on the boundary wall has begun, with Gate No 11 opening October 10. L&T employs 1,200 workers, facing challenges due to worker shortages during festivals. Security measures are being enhanced at the site.
headline: Ayodhya Ram Temple Trust Reveals Rs 1,400 Crore Spent on Construction

author: News18
summary: Maharashtra Governor CP Radhakrishnan has been elected as India’s Vice President, marking a win for the NDA. The results were announced on Tuesday, following strategic efforts by the BJP, including PM Modi. Radhakrishnan, who took office as Governor on July 31, 2024, previously served as Governor of Jharkhand and has a background in the BJP and RSS. Born on October 20, 1957, he has also represented the Lok Sabha and has a keen interest in sports.
headline: CP Radhakrishnan Elected India’s New Vice President, Strengthening NDA''s Position

author: News18
summary: Smoke rose from Kakadbhitta, Nepal, visible from Panitanki, India, amid anti-government protests. Security is heightened along the border, with SSB securing the bridge. Police and SSB are on high alert, conducting joint patrols. Nepalese PM KP Sharma Oli resigned due to unrest sparked by a social media ban. West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee urged peace along the border and advised journalists to avoid covering the situation in Nepal directly.
headline: Thick Smoke Seen From India-Nepal Bridge Amid Protests; Security Tightened

author: News18
summary: NDA nominee C P Radhakrishnan won the Vice President election, defeating B Sudershan Reddy by 152 votes. Radhakrishnan received 452 votes, while 15 were invalid. He is set to take the oath on September 12. Speculation arose over cross-voting as Radhakrishnan received 14 more votes than the NDA''s expected tally. Congratulatory messages poured in from leaders, including President Droupadi Murmu and Prime Minister Narendra Modi.
headline: C P Radhakrishnan Elected India''s 15th Vice President, Defeats Sudershan Reddy by 152 Votes

author: News18
summary: Three soldiers died in an avalanche at Siachen, the highest battlefield, in Ladakh. The incident occurred on Sunday at the 12,000-feet-high base camp, trapping Sepoy Mohit Kumar and Agniveers Niraj Kumar Choudhary and Dabhi Rakesh Devabhai. A rescue operation was launched, and their bodies were retrieved. The Indian Army honored their sacrifice in the line of duty.
headline: Avalanche Kills 3 Soldiers at Siachen Camp in Ladakh

author: News18
summary: In an age where climate change dominates headlines, three youngsters from Nalgonda district in Telangana have found a simple but powerful way to give back to nature—by turning everyday fruit seeds into forests.
headline: Nature’s Soldiers: How Three Friends In Telangana Are Fighting Climate Change With Seeds

author: News18
summary: The Gujarat High Court quashed an FIR against three in-laws accused of cruelty in a dowry case. Justice JC Doshi ruled on September 2, 2025, that the allegations were vague and lacked specifics. The 2016 FIR, filed at Vatva Police Station, cited IPC Sections 498A, 323, 294(1), 506(1), and Dowry Prohibition Act Sections 3 and 7. The court emphasized that vague claims do not warrant trial, highlighting the need for concrete evidence.
headline: Gujarat HC Quashes FIR Against Woman's In-Laws in Dowry Case

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court acquitted Rakesh Dutt Sharma, a doctor sentenced for killing an armed assailant. The court emphasized the right of private defense based on common sense, noting the deceased attacked Sharma first, armed with a pistol. The bench found that the trial and high courts failed to recognize the circumstances justifying Sharma''s actions. The ruling highlighted principles of self-defense, asserting that reasonable apprehension is sufficient to invoke this right.
headline: SC Acquits Doctor Who Killed Assailant in Self-Defense Case

author: News18
summary: During Pitru Paksha, Gokarna in Karnataka attracts devotees performing ancestral rituals. Families believe these rites free ancestors from suffering and bring peace. The town serves as a bridge between the living and the departed, with its sacred identity rooted in the Treta Yuga, linked to the warrior-sage Parashurama.
headline: Pitru Paksha in Gokarna: A Powerful Ancestral Rite Tradition

author: News18
summary: Voting for India''s next Vice President is underway using paper ballots instead of EVMs. This method ensures confidentiality and transparency as mandated by the Constitution. The current law requires paper ballots, which allow for ranking candidates and clear marking of preferences. Despite calls for EVMs to speed up the process, concerns over risks and the need for a parliamentary amendment have prevented their use. New ballot boxes are used for each election, ensuring security and integrity.
headline: Indian Vice Presidential Election Uses Ballots, Not EVMs

author: News18
summary: Stray dogs chased thieves, aiding police in arresting six and recovering 40 stolen motorcycles. The incident occurred Sunday after a man reported his bike stolen. The thieves fled but were blocked by dogs, leading to their capture. DCP Vishwajeet Srivastava confirmed the thieves admitted to stealing 40 vehicles. The arrested include Junaid (24), Aras (21), and others, with a 16-year-old also detained.
headline: Stray Dogs Aid UP Police in Catching Thieves, Recover 40 Stolen Bikes

author: News18
summary: Actor Darshan, accused in the Renukaswamy murder case, told a court that jail conditions are unbearable. He described health issues and requested poison, stating, “I can’t live like this anymore.” The judge responded that such requests cannot be fulfilled. Darshan was arrested in June 2024 for the murder of Renukaswamy and remains in custody after bail was revoked due to witness tampering concerns. Court proceedings continue with other accused as well.
headline: Darshan Complains About Jail Conditions, Desires '

author: News18
summary: As unrest continues in Nepal, some Indians are stranded in Kathmandu. Telugu families were moved to a guest house for safety as agitators gathered at their hotel. Violence erupted following the resignations of President Ramchandra Paudel and Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli, with demonstrators setting fire to key government buildings. Eyewitnesses reported serious injuries to the wife of former prime minister Jhala Nath Khanal during the chaos.
headline: Indians Stranded in Kathmandu Amid Ongoing Unrest in Nepal

author: News18
summary: India heightened security along its border with Nepal after PM KP Sharma Oli resigned amid protests against a revoked social media ban. Paramilitary forces patrol West Bengal''s Panitanki, while trade is partially halted. The Indian foreign ministry advised caution for nationals in Nepal, noting curfews in several cities. The Sashastra Seema Bal intensified vigilance, ensuring adequate deployment along the 1,751 km border.
headline: India Alerts Border Areas as Nepal PM Resigns Amid Violent Protests

author: News18
summary: Congress MLA Chander Shekhar Thakur began an indefinite protest against NHAI on Monday, citing negligence in National Highway-3 construction. He claims locals have suffered for years, demanding accountability and relief. Thakur criticized the slow progress and poor conditions of the highway, stating officials ignored his concerns. He vowed to continue the sit-in until a solution is found, invoking Mahatma Gandhi''s Satyagraha movement.
headline: Himachal MLA Protests NHAI Over Highway Negligence, Launches Indefinite Sit-in

author: News18
summary: The September 9 Vice Presidential election recalls Indira Gandhi''s 1969 cross-voting, defying Congress. After President Dr Zakir Hussain''s death, she supported independent candidate V V Giri over the party''s choice, Neelam Sanjiva Reddy. This gamble led to Giri''s narrow victory and ultimately caused a split in Congress, solidifying Gandhi''s power and reshaping the party''s future.
headline: Indira Gandhi's 1969 Cross-Voting in Vice-Presidential Election

author: News18
summary: The Delhi High Court dismissed a plea to quash a POCSO case, stating the accused must bear the stigma of sexual assault, not the survivor. The case involves a man accused of exploiting a minor girl through blackmail. Justice Girish Kathpalia rejected claims of a settlement, emphasizing that the survivor should not face shame. The court noted that private agreements cannot diminish the seriousness of such offenses and imposed a Rs 10,000 cost on the accused.
headline: Delhi High Court: Stigma of Rape Lies with Accused, Not Survivor

author: News18
summary: In a shocking and devastating turn of events, conservative political activist Charlie Kirk was fatally shot while delivering a speech at Utah Valley University. The incident occurred during a packed hall event as part of Kirk’s “American Comeback Tour.” n18oc_world News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube
headline: BREAKING: Charlie Kirk Killed On Campus, Students Flee In Terror After Shooting | Trump | 4K

author: News18
summary: After an Israeli attack on Doha, Netanyahu likened it to the US response post-9/11, calling October 7 Israel''s 9/11. He accused Qatar of harboring terrorists and warned nations to expel them or face action. The Israeli strike occurred as Hamas leaders met in Doha for ceasefire talks. Qatar condemned the attack as a violation of international law. Netanyahu stated that nations hosting terrorists must be held accountable amid ongoing military actions in Gaza and beyond.
headline: Netanyahu Defends Doha Strike, Compares Hamas to US Post-9/11 Response

author: News18
summary: Benny Johnson recalled how Charlie Kirk helped his family after their home was damaged by fire and threats. Kirk provided them shelter in Florida during a crisis. Johnson praised Kirk, who was shot dead in what officials call a “political assassination.” President Trump condemned the killing, calling it a “dark moment for America” and hailing Kirk as a “martyr for truth.”
headline: Charlie Kirk Remembered as a Martyr by MAGA Supporter Benny Johnson

author: News18
summary: After the shooting of Charlie Kirk at Utah Valley University, conflicting statements from the FBI and Utah State Police have created chaos. The FBI stated the shooter was in custody, while Utah police claimed the suspect escaped. Governor Spencer Cox confirmed a “person of interest” was held, but not the shooter, leading to public uncertainty.
headline: Confusion Over Charlie Kirk Shooting: FBI, Utah Police at Odds

author: News18
summary: Former VP Jagdeep Dhankhar congratulated CP Radhakrishnan on winning the Vice President election. Dhankhar expressed confidence in Radhakrishnan''s leadership, stating it would bring honor and glory to the office. Radhakrishnan won by 152 votes against B Sudershan Reddy, with 452 lawmakers voting for him. The election followed Dhankhar''s resignation on July 21 due to health reasons.
headline: Jagdeep Dhankhar Congratulates New VP CP Radhakrishnan After Election Victory

author: News18
summary: The Madhya Pradesh High Court dismissed a woman''s appeal against a man''s acquittal in a rape case, stating no deception occurred as she remained legally married. The court noted her inconsistent statements and reliance on precedents distinguishing consensual sex from rape based on false marriage promises. The judges found no errors in the trial court''s evidence evaluation, leading to the dismissal of the appeal.
headline: MP HC Upholds Acquittal in False Promise Rape Case

author: News18
summary: The MEA advised Indian citizens in Nepal to remain indoors and avoid travel due to ongoing violent protests. Citizens should follow local safety instructions and contact the Embassy for assistance. The advisory comes after intense protests against the Nepal government, resulting in casualties. Bangladesh has issued similar warnings for its nationals in Nepal.
headline: MEA Advises Indians in Nepal to Stay Indoors Amid Protests

author: News18
summary: PM Modi announced a Rs 1,500 crore relief package for Himachal Pradesh after catastrophic rains. He reviewed damage in Dharamshala, conducted an aerial survey, and held a meeting with CM Sukhu. The aid includes Rs 2 lakh for deceased families and Rs 50,000 for injured. PM Modi emphasized restoring infrastructure and education, while the ongoing monsoon has claimed 370 lives and blocked 744 roads, with Kullu being the most affected.
headline: PM Modi Announces Rs 1,500 Crore Aid for Himachal Flood Relief

author: News18
summary: Parvez Alam spent over four years in jail without charges in a dowry death case. On September 4, the Allahabad High Court ordered his release on bail, emphasizing the right to a speedy trial. Justice Krishan Pahal noted that prolonged incarceration without charges violates constitutional rights. The court directed Alam''s release on a personal bond, with conditions to ensure he does not tamper with evidence or intimidate witnesses.
headline: HC Orders Release of Man Held 4 Years Without Charges in Dowry Death Case

author: News18
summary: IndiGo and Air India suspended all flights to Nepal amid unrest, closing Kathmandu’s airport. Passengers can seek refunds or book alternate flights. The airlines are monitoring the situation. Violent protests have resulted in 19 deaths and injuries, prompting the resignation of Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli. Air India offers a waiver on rescheduling charges for tickets to Kathmandu until 11 September.
headline: IndiGo, Air India Halt Flights to Nepal Due to Unrest, Issue Advisories

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court stated that while a certificate is needed for electronic evidence under the Customs Act, it need not strictly comply with Section 138C(4). The court partly allowed an appeal against a CESTAT ruling that had set aside penalties for alleged duty evasion. It noted that compliance with Section 138C(4) was met through existing records and statements under Section 108, emphasizing that the authenticity of documents was not in dispute.
headline: Supreme Court: Certificate Not Always Required for Electronic Evidence Under Customs Act

author: News18
summary: The Supreme Court stated that review jurisdiction cannot be assumed without legal authority. Justice Ahsanuddin Amanullah emphasized that review is distinct from appellate power and must adhere to Order 47, Rule 1 of CPC. The court outlined that review is limited to correcting mistakes, not substituting views, and cannot be used as an appeal. The bench set aside a high court order for exceeding review jurisdiction and directed the trial court to expedite pending applications.
headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Limitations on Review Jurisdiction

author: News18
summary: The Kerala High Court ruled that those paying for sex at brothels can be prosecuted under the ITP Act. Justice VG Arun emphasized sex workers cannot be viewed as commodities, noting many are victims of human trafficking. A petitioner sought to quash charges after a police raid, claiming he was merely using sex worker services. The court quashed some charges but upheld prosecution under specific sections of the ITP Act.
headline: HC: Customers at Brothels Can Be Prosecuted Under ITP Act

author: News18
summary: The Yamuna has risen to unprecedented levels, displacing over 4,200 residents in Mathura and Vrindavan. Temples are inundated, and roads are submerged. '
created_by: '" said a local. The situation is dire
headline: with floodwaters reaching the Taj Mahal for the first time since 1978. Relief camps are set up
image_url: but conditions are poor. Officials warn of more evacuations as rain is forecasted."
source_url: Yamuna Floods Displace Thousands in Lord Krishna's Sacred City

author: News18
summary: A report reveals that while the average marriage age for girls in India is now 22.9 years, states like West Bengal, Jharkhand, and Chhattisgarh still see girls marrying before 18. Nationally, 2.1% of girls marry under 18, with 25% marrying between 18-20. West Bengal has the highest rate of early marriages at 6.3%, while Kerala has the lowest at 0.1%. The average age in rural areas is 22.4 years, compared to 24.3 years in urban areas.
headline: Child Marriage Still Occurs in India, Particularly in Certain States

author: News18
summary: The Vice Presidential election on September 9 features NDA''s CP Radhakrishnan vs. INDIA''s B Sudarshan Reddy. Unlike typical votes, no whip is issued, allowing MPs to vote freely. Reddy urged MPs to use discretion, while the NDA aims to prevent defections. With 776 eligible voters, Radhakrishnan needs 349 votes to win. Past elections show cross voting can alter outcomes, despite the NDA''s numerical advantage. Internal party penalties may follow cross voting.
headline: Vice-Presidential Election: Cross-Voting Risks Without Whip Enforcement

author: News18
summary: House Speaker Mike Johnson delivered a deeply emotional address after the shocking assassination of conservative activist Charlie Kirk at Utah Valley University. In his speech, Johnson described the atrocity as an attack not just on Kirk but on the values of democracy, free speech, and public safety itself. n18oc_world News18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube
headline: Charlie Kirk Killed In Utah: Mike Johnson’s Emotional Address on Kirk’s Death | US News | Trump | 4K

author: News18
summary: In today''s digest, Gen-Z protests in Nepal defy curfew on Day 2, with 19 killed over a social media ban. PM Modi votes in vice-presidential elections, while Australian PM criticizes a senator''s anti-India remarks. Delhi’s Maulana Azad Medical College received a bomb threat, and 16-year-old Abhimanyu Mishra defeats world champion D Gukesh in chess. Raj Kundra summoned in a Rs 60.48 crore fraud case.
headline: Protests in Nepal, Vice Presidential Elections in India, and More News Highlights

author: News18
summary: A bomb threat email to the Delhi CM’s Secretariat on Tuesday warned of a detonation at 3:30 pm. The Bomb Disposal Squad was deployed, and security measures were heightened. Police noted similarities to previous hoaxes and are tracing the email''s origin. Senior officers oversaw safety checks, and intensive searches are ongoing at both the Secretariat and Maulana Azad Medical College. Authorities are treating the threat seriously, despite indications it may have been misdirected.
headline: Bomb Threat Email at Delhi CM Secretariat Triggers Panic, Likely Hoax

author: News18
summary: NDA’s CP Radhakrishnan received 427 votes in the Vice President election, following Jagdeep Dhankhar''s resignation. A total of 767 votes were cast, with 752 valid. The NDA nominee''s 452 votes surpassed the INDIA bloc''s 300. Polling occurred at Parliament House from 10 am to 5 pm. Some regional parties abstained, reflecting state-level priorities and distancing from both NDA and Congress-led INDIA bloc.
headline: CP Radhakrishnan Elected India’s Next Vice President, Oath Likely on Sept 12

author: News18

author: Deepak Verma
summary:
नई दिल्ली: एनडीए उम्मीदवार और महाराष्ट्र के राज्यपाल रहे सी.पी. राधाकृष्णन भारत के नए उपराष्ट्रपति चुने गए हैं. राज्यसभा महासचिव पीसी मोदी ने बताया कि उन्हें 452 प्रथम वरीयता मत मिले. वहीं विपक्ष के उम्मीदवार जस्टिस सुदर्शन रेड्डी को 300 प्रथम वरीयता मत प्राप्त हुए. इस तरह स्पष्ट अंतर से राधाकृष्णन की जीत पक्की हुई. राधाकृष्णन के नामांकन के बाद से ही एनडीए खेमे का पलड़ा भारी माना जा रहा था. उनके चुने जाने के साथ ही अब वे राज्यसभा के सभापति पद की जिम्मेदारी भी संभालेंगे.
प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी ने X पर लिखा, ‘सी.पी. राधाकृष्णन जी को 2025 के उपराष्ट्रपति चुनाव में विजयी होने पर बधाई. उनका जीवन सदैव समाज सेवा और गरीबों व वंचितों को सशक्त बनाने के लिए समर्पित रहा है. मुझे विश्वास है कि वे एक उत्कृष्ट उपराष्ट्रपति होंगे, जो हमारे संवैधानिक मूल्यों को सुदृढ़ करेंगे और संसदीय संवाद को आगे बढ़ाएंगे.’
बीजेपी के सभी सांसद मंगलवार रात करीब 9.30 बजे केंद्रीय मंत्री प्रहलाद जोशी के आवास पर मिलेंगे. वे यहां नवनिर्वाचित उपराष्ट्रपति सीपी राधाकृष्णन का स्वागत करेंगे. इस मौके पर प्रधानमंत्री नरेंद्र मोदी भी राधाकृष्णन को बधाई देने पहुंचेंगे.
उपराष्ट्रपति चुनाव 2025: विपक्षी उम्मीदवार ने क्या कहा?
नतीजों के बाद विपक्षी उम्मीदवार बी सुदर्शन रेड्डी ने कहा, ‘हमारे महान गणराज्य की लोकतांत्रिक प्रक्रियाओं में अटूट विश्वास के साथ मैं विनम्रतापूर्वक परिणाम स्वीकार करता हूं.’ उन्होंने लिखा कि ‘हालांकि परिणाम मेरे पक्ष में नहीं है, लेकिन वैचारिक संघर्ष और भी जोरदार तरीके से जारी है.’

Vice President Election 2025: NDA की जीत, Congress को बड़ा झटका | Aar Paar With Amish Devgan#VPElection2025 #VicePresidentElection #ElectionResults #BreakingNews | @AMISHDEVGAN pic.twitter.com/9h1G248T6V
— News18 India (@News18India) September 9, 2025

किसने की क्रॉस-वोटिंग?
INDIA गठबंधन ने दावा किया था कि उसके उम्मीदवार को 315 वोट मिलेंगे. इसका मतलब है कि लगभग 14-15 विपक्षी सांसदों ने शायद क्रॉस वोट किया है.
जयराम रमेश बोले- विपक्ष की हार नहीं, नैतिक जीत हुई है
कांग्रेस के वरिष्ठ नेता जयराम रमेश ने एक्स पर लिखा कि इस चुनाव में विपक्ष पूरी तरह से एकजुट रहा और उसका प्रदर्शन ‘सम्मानजनक’ रहा. उन्होंने बताया कि विपक्ष के साझा उम्मीदवार जस्टिस (सेवानिवृत्त) बी. सुदर्शन रेड्डी को करीब 40% वोट मिले. जबकि 2022 के उपराष्ट्रपति चुनाव में विपक्ष को महज 26% वोट ही मिले थे. रमेश ने लिखा, ‘यह दिखाता है कि विपक्ष का आधार मजबूत हो रहा है.’

The Opposition stood united for the Vice Presidential election.
Its performance has undeniably been most respectable.
Its joint candidate Justice (retd) B. Sudershan Reddy secured 40% of the vote. In 2022, the Opposition had received 26% of the vote in the Vice Presidential…
— Jairam Ramesh (@Jairam_Ramesh) September 9, 2025

जयराम रमेश ने कहा कि भाजपा की अंकगणितीय जीत वास्तव में एक नैतिक और राजनीतिक हार है. उन्होंने जोर देकर कहा कि विचारधारा की लड़ाई अभी भी पूरी मजबूती से जारी है और इसे और आगे ले जाया जाएगा.
headline: सीपी राधाकृष्णन होंगे अगले उपराष्ट्रपति, 15 विपक्षी सांसदों ने की क्रॉस-वोटिंग

author: News18
summary: Poland''s military reported drone violations of its airspace amid a Russian attack on Ukraine. Four airports, including Chopin Airport, were closed due to military activity. Poland scrambled air defenses and urged residents in Podlaskie, Mazowieckie, and Lublin to stay home. Defence Minister Wladyslaw Kosiniak-Kamysz confirmed Polish aircraft engaged hostile objects. The situation follows warnings from President Karol Nawrocki about potential Russian aggression.
headline: Poland Reports Russian Drones Breaching Airspace, Closes Airports

author: News18
summary: The Nepali Army arrested 26 individuals for looting during youth-led protests following Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli''s resignation. The arrests occurred in Kathmandu and Bhaktapur, with five linked to a bank heist and 21 to violent incidents. The Army urged citizens to cooperate with security forces and report suspicious activities, emphasizing the need for community vigilance amid unrest sparked by a ban on 26 social media platforms.
headline: Nepal Army Arrests 26 Amid Gen-Z Protests Over Looting and Vandalism

author: News18
summary: White House Trade Advisor Peter Navarro criticized Indians fact-checking his posts on X regarding Russian oil. He claimed, “India’s keyboard minions are hijacking X’s Community Notes to bury the facts.” Navarro argued India''s tariffs harm American jobs and that its oil purchases support Putin’s war. He warned that India must negotiate on trade or face consequences, highlighting India’s high tariffs compared to other countries.
headline: Navarro Criticizes India’s Oil Purchases and Community Notes on X

author: News18
summary: US President Donald Trump confirmed on Tuesday that trade negotiations with India are resuming. In a Truth Social post, he expressed optimism about addressing trade barriers and mentioned his friendship with PM Modi. While not officially confirmed, Trump''s statement aligns with increasing US-India cooperation in defense and technology. Trade tensions have existed since 2018, with efforts to reach a deal stalled over key issues.
headline: Trump Announces Resumption of US-India Trade Talks with PM Modi

author: News18
summary: ICE agents arrested 475 at a Hyundai plant in Georgia, including over 300 South Korean nationals. South Korea''s Chief of Staff stated the US agreed to charter a flight for their return. Experts warn the raid may harm US foreign investment.
headline: US-South Korea Relations Strained After ICE Raid at Hyundai Plant

author: News18
summary: Qatari PM Al-Thani condemned an Israeli missile strike on Doha as a “clear violation” of sovereignty. He called it a “terrorist act” against ongoing ceasefire talks with Hamas. Al-Thani stated Qatar reserves the right to respond and criticized claims of prior warning from the US. He accused Israeli PM Netanyahu of provoking instability and reaffirmed Qatar''s commitment to mediating peace. Casualties were reported, and Al-Thani praised security forces for their quick response.
headline: Qatari PM Warns Israel of Retaliation After Missile Strike in Doha

author: News18
summary: The DOJ will prosecute Decarlos Brown for fatally stabbing Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte train. The attack occurred on August 22, captured by surveillance, with no passengers intervening. Brown faces federal charges for causing death on a mass transit system and a state charge of first-degree murder. Attorney General Bondi condemned the murder as a result of failed crime policies. Zarutska had moved to the US three years ago, seeking a better life.
headline: Federal Charges Filed Against Man Who Murdered Ukrainian Refugee in Charlotte

author: News18
summary: Trump condemned the Israeli strike in Doha, calling it “unfortunate” and placing blame on Netanyahu. He emphasized Qatar''s ally status and said he had assured its leaders that such attacks wouldn''t happen again. Trump was informed by the US military and instructed a warning to Qatar, but it arrived too late. He also condemned Hamas and reiterated the need for peace, stating the strike could be an opportunity for it.
headline: Trump Blames Netanyahu for Qatar Strike, Denies US Involvement

author: News18
summary: Donald Trump urged the EU to impose up to 100% tariffs on India and China to pressure Russia''s Putin. This request was made during a call with EU sanctions envoy David O’Sullivan. The US indicated it might impose similar tariffs if the EU complies. The EU has preferred sanctions over tariffs to isolate Russia, but discussions on secondary sanctions against India and China for buying Russian oil are ongoing.
headline: Trump Urges EU to Impose 100% Tariffs on India, China to Pressure Russia

author: News18
summary: Kathmandu is in revolt, with protesters calling for a civilian-led interim government as the political order collapses. A senior bureaucrat noted a split among protesters, some favoring a former chief justice to lead. The army remains cautious, and experts see parallels to Bangladesh''s political upheaval. The demand for an interim government is now seen as essential for Nepal''s future, as the old order appears finished.
headline: Nepal Protests Demand Civilian-Led Interim Government Amid Political Chaos

author: News18
summary: The US Supreme Court will fast-track an appeal on Trump''s tariffs, set for the first week of November. The case challenges tariffs imposed during his administration, including a 10% baseline tariff on imports and 25% on selected goods from Canada, China, and Mexico. The appeals court ruled Trump overstepped his authority, potentially affecting $750 billion to $1 trillion in tariffs if deemed illegal.
headline: US Supreme Court to Hear Trump's Tariffs Appeal in November

author: News18
summary: Poland''s military activated defensive procedures after a Russian aerial attack on September 9–10. The Rzeszów-Jasionka Airport closed temporarily due to unplanned military activity. US F-35s were spotted in the area, and NATO increased its aerial presence. US Rep. Joe Wilson called the drone incident an '
headline: Polish Airport Closed as Russian Drones Enter NATO Airspace

author: News18
summary: The BLS reports the US job market added 911,000 fewer jobs than last year, signaling a slowdown. Monthly growth averaged 106,000 in 2024 and just 44,000 in 2025. Major losses occurred in tech, with the information industry down 88,000 jobs. Other sectors like leisure and hospitality also saw significant revisions. These figures intensify pressure on the Federal Reserve to reduce interest rates amid concerns over President Trump''s economic policies.
headline: US Job Market Loses 911,000 Jobs; Federal Reserve Faces Pressure to Cut Rates

summary: French President Emmanuel Macron appointed Defence Minister Sebastien Lecornu as prime minister, replacing Francois Bayrou after his dismissal following a confidence vote. Lecornu, 39, is tasked with leading cross-party consultations for a new national budget. He is the only minister to remain since Macron''s first term began in 2017 and faces challenges in garnering support from rivals in parliament.
headline: Macron Appoints Sebastien Lecornu as New Prime Minister

summary: Nepal Army Chief General Ashok Raj Sigdel urged dialogue in a video message amid ongoing protests. He called for the suspension of demonstrations, emphasizing national unity and the need to safeguard the nation''s interests. Sigdel expressed sorrow over the violence and loss of life, urging citizens to uphold peace and not harm national heritage. His address followed the resignations of PM KP Sharma Oli and President Ram Chandra Poudel amid escalating unrest and protests against a social media ban.
headline: Nepal Army Chief Calls for Peace Amid Gen Z Protests in National Address

summary: President Trump did not support Israel''s airstrikes against Hamas leaders in Qatar, the White House said. Israel targeted senior Hamas figures in Doha, including a chief negotiator. While an Israeli official claimed Trump approved the strike, the White House denied it. The attack raised international concerns, with leaders like UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and UN Secretary-General António Guterres condemning it. Hamas confirmed six deaths, including a leader''s son, while Netanyahu took full responsibility.
headline: Trump Opposes Israeli Strikes on Qatar, White House Clarifies

author: News18
summary: Elisabet Lann collapsed on stage shortly after her appointment as Health Minister. Eyewitnesses reported she lost consciousness while stepping back after her remarks. Security and Prime Minister Kristersson rushed to assist her. Lann later attributed the incident to low blood sugar and returned briefly to address attendees. Q&A sessions were canceled, raising concerns about her ability to manage the role amid scrutiny of Sweden''s healthcare system. No updates on her condition have been released.
headline: Sweden’s New Health Minister Faints During Press Conference

author: News18
summary: Yashoda Adhikari of PBS Radio Nepal sought help while trapped in her office during violent protests. Anti-government demonstrations by “Gen Z” escalated, leading to arson and vandalism in Kathmandu. Yashoda''s plea for assistance prompted the Nepal Army to rescue her and colleagues. Following the turmoil, Prime Minister KP Sharma Oli and President Ram Chandra Poudel resigned amid widespread unrest and clashes resulting in fatalities.
headline: Nepal Journalist Rescued Amid Violent Protests and Fires in Kathmandu

author: News18
summary: Qatar’s Interior Ministry confirmed that Corporal Bader Saad Mohammed al-Humaidi al-Dosari was killed in an Israeli airstrike in Doha targeting Hamas leaders. The ministry described him as “martyred” in the first known military strike on Qatari territory, which also injured several personnel. The attack has drawn international condemnation for violating Qatari sovereignty and international law. Further details on the injured or damage are pending.
headline: Qatar Confirms Security Member Killed in Israeli Strike on Doha

summary: Nepal faces political turmoil as PM KP Sharma Oli and President Ram Chandra Poudel resign after violent protests. Triggered by a ban on social media, the unrest led to at least 19 deaths on September 8. Protesters demanded accountability for corruption, targeting political figures'' homes. The military called for peace amid uncertainty, as the Indian government advised its citizens to avoid travel to Nepal. The future remains uncertain with ongoing protests for reform.
headline: Nepal's PM and President Resign Amid Gen-Z Protests and Violence
